Use a **LiveComponent** when you have a discrete interactive region that owns state: a multi-step form, a sortable list, a filterable panel, a modal. The parent passes props in via `assigns`, and the component mutates its own socket via `handle_event/3`. See §5. Use a **LiveView** only at the top level — one per route. The LiveView is the process. It owns the URL, the session, and coordinates child LiveComponents. You should almost never have a LiveView nested inside another LiveView (that's what LiveComponents are for). ### The "thin parent, stateful children" rule — worked example Consider a page at `/teams/:id` with a team header, an editable member list, a filter panel, and an add-member modal. The wrong shape: # BAD: everything in the parent defmodule MyAppWeb.TeamLive do def mount(_, _, socket) do {:ok, assign(socket, team: nil, members: [], filter: "", modal_open?: false, form: nil)} end def handle_event("filter", %{"q" => q}, socket), do: ... def handle_event("open_modal", _, socket), do: ... def handle_event("add_member", %{"member" => attrs}, socket), do: ... def handle_event("remove_member", %{"id" => id}, socket), do: ... def handle_event("validate", %{"member" => attrs}, socket), do: ... # ...12 more clauses end The right shape: defmodule MyAppWeb.TeamLive do use MyAppWeb, :live_view def mount(%{"id" => id}, _session, socket) do {:ok, socket |> assign_async(:team, fn -> {:ok, %{team: MyApp.Teams.get!(id)}} end)} end def render(assigns) do ~H"""
<.live_component module={TeamHeader} id={"team-#{@team.result.id}"} team={@team.result} /> <.live_component module={MemberList} id={"members-#{@team.result.id}"} team={@team.result} /> <.live_component module={AddMemberModal} id="add-member-modal" team={@team.result} />
""" end end Each child is its own `Phoenix.LiveComponent` with focused state and a single responsibility. See §5 for the discipline inside each component. ## 3. HEEx template reference HEEx is Phoenix's typed, compile-time-validated template language. The `~H` sigil parses the template at compile time and raises on malformed tags, unknown components, or unresolved attrs. ### 3.1 Verified routes with `~p` Every internal route uses the `~p` sigil. The router's `MyAppWeb.Router` compiles a set of allowed paths at build time; any `~p"/typo"` that doesn't match a declared route raises a compile-time warning. # Static route ~p"/users" # With a path param — interpolated value is URL-encoded automatically ~p"/users/#{@user}" # implements String.Chars or Phoenix.Param ~p"/users/#{@user.id}" # explicit id # With query params as a map ~p"/users?#{[sort: "name", page: 2]}" # renders: /users?sort=name&page=2 # Merging query params from an existing map params = %{sort: "name", status: "active"} ~p"/users?#{params}" # Query params can also be a keyword list ~p"/search?#{[q: "phoenix", limit: 20]}" The interpolation respects `Phoenix.Param`, so passing a struct (like `%User{id: 3}`) resolves to the right path segment. This is why you can write `~p"/users/#{@user}"` and not `~p"/users/#{@user.id}"`. ### 3.2 HEEx directives — `:for` and `:if` Replace legacy `<%= for %>` / `<%= if %>` with native directives:
  • <%= user.name %>
Hello, <%= @current_user.name %>
Directives can combine: <%= user.name %> # With a block form for nested iteration + conditional

<%= group.name %>

<%= member.name %>

The directive form is strictly more optimized than `<%= for %>`. LiveView's change-tracker can diff the iterated list item-by-item; the Eex form forces a full re-render of the surrounding block. ### 3.3 Class lists HEEx supports a class list literal — a list of `{condition, class}` tuples, or a mix of strings and lists:
Click me
Falsy entries (`nil`, `false`) are filtered out. No more string concatenation or `Enum.join`. ### 3.4 The `<.link>` component `<.link>` is the universal navigation primitive, replacing `live_link`, `live_patch`, `live_redirect`, and raw `` for internal routes. # Cross-LiveView navigation (old live_redirect/live_link) <.link navigate={~p"/users/#{@user}"}>View user # Same-LiveView URL patch (old live_patch) <.link patch={~p"/users?sort=#{@sort}"}>Sort # External link or full-page reload <.link href="https://example.com">External <.link href={~p"/logout"} method="delete">Log out See §11 for the full navigate-vs-patch decision tree. ### 3.5 The `~H` sigil Every template body inside a module uses `~H`, not `~E` or `~h`: def render(assigns) do ~H"""

Hello, <%= @name %>

""" end The triple-quoted heredoc is the idiomatic form. `~H` is what enables compile-time validation — a typo in an attr name or a missing component will raise during `mix compile`, not at runtime. ## 4. Function components + slots A function component is a plain Elixir function with a single `assigns` argument that returns HEEx. Its job is to render. It has no socket, no database, no `case` statements on business logic. ### 4.1 `attr` declarations Every input to a function component must be declared with `attr`. This gives you compile-time validation, IDE hints, and documentation. attr :name, :string, required: true attr :variant, :atom, values: [:primary, :secondary, :danger], default: :primary attr :disabled, :boolean, default: false attr :rest, :global, include: ~w(form name) def button(assigns) do ~H""" """ end Key attr options: - `:required` — raises at compile time if omitted - `:default` — used when the caller doesn't pass the attr - `:values` — an enumerated constraint (compile-time checked against literal attrs) - `:rest, :global` — collects undeclared attrs into a map, passes to HTML via `{@rest}`. The `include:` list whitelists additional global attrs like `form=` or `name=`. ### 4.2 Slots Slots are named child-content regions. Every function component gets a free `:inner_block` slot; declare additional slots explicitly. slot :inner_block, required: true slot :actions slot :footer def card(assigns) do ~H"""
<%= render_slot(@inner_block) %>
<%= render_slot(@actions) %>
<%= render_slot(@footer) %>
""" end Usage: <.card>

Main content here.

<:actions> <.button name="Save" /> <.button name="Cancel" variant={:secondary} /> <:footer> Last updated <%= @updated_at %> ### 4.3 `:let` — passing data from the component to the slot When the component wants to parameterize a slot (e.g., a table row renderer that needs the row data), use `:let`: slot :col, required: true do attr :label, :string, required: true end attr :rows, :list, required: true def table(assigns) do ~H"""
<%= col.label %>
<%= render_slot(col, row) %>
""" end Usage — the caller receives each row via `:let={row}`: <.table rows={@users}> <:col :let={user} label="Name"><%= user.name %> <:col :let={user} label="Email"><%= user.email %> <:col :let={user} label="Actions"> <.link navigate={~p"/users/#{user}"}>View ### 4.4 Worked example — polymorphic card attr :title, :string, required: true attr :variant, :atom, values: [:info, :warning, :danger, :success], default: :info attr :icon, :string, default: nil slot :inner_block, required: true slot :footer def alert_card(assigns) do ~H"""

<%= @title %>

<%= render_slot(@inner_block) %>
<%= render_slot(@footer) %>
""" end If a decision is needed — e.g., a badge color for a status — **compute it in the parent**, not in the component: # BAD — branching inside the function component def status_badge(assigns) do ~H""" "bg-green" :pending -> "bg-yellow" :inactive -> "bg-gray" end }> <%= @status %> """ end # GOOD — parent computes the class, passes as attr def mount(_, _, socket) do {:ok, assign(socket, :badge_class, badge_class_for(user.status))} end # In the component: attr :class, :string, required: true attr :label, :string, required: true def status_badge(assigns) do ~H""" <%= @label %> """ end ## 5. LiveComponents A `Phoenix.LiveComponent` is a stateful child of a LiveView. It shares the parent LiveView's process, but has its own socket assigns, its own `handle_event/3`, and its own render function. The LiveView process owns the component's state, but the component has exclusive write access to it. ### 5.1 The `update/2` discipline This is the most important rule in this section, and the easiest to get wrong. **`update/2` is the ONLY function that calls `assign(socket, :prop, assigns.prop)` for external data.** If you assign a prop somewhere else — `handle_event`, `handle_info`, a helper function — you break the component's lifecycle contract. Why? When the parent LiveView re-renders, LiveView calls `update/2` on every child LiveComponent with the new assigns. If you need any derived state (a filtered list, a computed label, a sorted index), compute it right there, in the same pass. Later mutations by the component itself (toggling `:open?`, selecting a row) happen in `handle_event`, and they only touch component-local state — never the props. defmodule MyAppWeb.FilterPanel do use MyAppWeb, :live_component def update(%{users: users, query: q} = assigns, socket) do visible = Enum.filter(users, &String.contains?(&1.name, q)) {:ok, socket |> assign(assigns) |> assign(:visible, visible) |> assign_new(:open?, fn -> false end)} end def handle_event("toggle", _, socket) do {:noreply, assign(socket, :open?, not socket.assigns.open?)} end def render(assigns) do ~H"""
  • <%= user.name %>
""" end end Note `assign_new(:open?, fn -> false end)` — this sets a default only on first mount so subsequent re-renders don't clobber the user's toggle state. ### 5.2 `phx-target={@myself}` — routing events to the component By default, `phx-click="toggle"` sends the event to the parent LiveView. To route it to the component itself, add `phx-target={@myself}`. The `@myself` assign is a magic assign LiveView injects into every component — a CID (component id) that LiveView uses internally. Without `phx-target={@myself}`, the parent's `handle_event/3` runs, and the component never sees the event. ### 5.3 `send_update/2` — parent pushing into a child When the parent needs to push a prop change mid-lifecycle, call `send_update/2`. This causes LiveView to invoke the child's `update/2` with the new assigns merged in. # In the parent LiveView's handle_info/2: def handle_info({:new_user, user}, socket) do send_update(MyAppWeb.UserList, id: "user-list-main", new_user: user) {:noreply, socket} end Important: `send_update/2` is partial — the assigns you pass are merged into the existing ones. If you want to replace a list entirely, pass the whole new list. ### 5.4 Child-to-parent signaling LiveComponents cannot directly return a message to the parent. Instead, send a message to `self()` (which is the LiveView's process) and handle it in the parent's `handle_info/2`: # In the component: def handle_event("selected", %{"id" => id}, socket) do send(self(), {:user_selected, id}) {:noreply, socket} end # In the parent LiveView: def handle_info({:user_selected, id}, socket) do {:noreply, push_patch(socket, to: ~p"/users/#{id}")} end Include `@myself` in the message if the parent needs to reply via `send_update/2`: send(self(), {:toggle_sidebar, @myself}) ### 5.5 The `:id` requirement Every `<.live_component />` call **must include `id:`**. Without it, the component becomes stateless — LiveView rebuilds its assigns from scratch on every parent render, and `handle_event` state vanishes between interactions. # CORRECT <.live_component module={MyAppWeb.FilterPanel} id="filter-main" users={@users} query={@q} /> # BROKEN — no id, state resets on every parent render <.live_component module={MyAppWeb.FilterPanel} users={@users} query={@q} /> Use a stable id — derive it from the resource id (`"user-form-#{user.id}"`) or use a constant string if there's only one instance on the page (`"filter-main"`). ## 6. Forms + nested associations Forms in Phoenix 1.7+ always flow through `Phoenix.Component.to_form/2`. You build a changeset, wrap it in a form struct, assign it to the socket, and pass it to the `<.form>` component. For nested associations (parent with many children that can be added and removed dynamically), use `cast_assoc` with `:sort_param` and `:drop_param`. ### 6.1 Simple form # In the context: def change_user(user, attrs \\ %{}) do User.changeset(user, attrs) end # In the LiveComponent: def update(%{user: user} = assigns, socket) do form = Accounts.change_user(user) |> to_form() {:ok, socket |> assign(assigns) |> assign(:form, form)} end def handle_event("validate", %{"user" => params}, socket) do changeset = socket.assigns.user |> Accounts.change_user(params) |> Map.put(:action, :validate) {:noreply, assign(socket, :form, to_form(changeset))} end def handle_event("save", %{"user" => params}, socket) do case Accounts.update_user(socket.assigns.user, params) do {:ok, user} -> send(self(), {:user_updated, user}) {:noreply, assign(socket, :form, Accounts.change_user(user) |> to_form())} {:error, changeset} -> {:noreply, assign(socket, :form, to_form(changeset))} end end def render(assigns) do ~H""" <.form for={@form} phx-change="validate" phx-submit="save" phx-target={@myself}> <.input field={@form[:name]} label="Name" /> <.input field={@form[:email]} type="email" label="Email" /> <.button type="submit">Save """ end ### 6.2 Nested has_many — the `cast_assoc` pattern Consider a `Team` that has_many `Members`, and you want a single form that creates or edits the team and all its members at once, with add/remove buttons for each member row. # Schema: schema "teams" do field :name, :string has_many :members, Member, on_replace: :delete timestamps() end def changeset(team, attrs) do team |> cast(attrs, [:name]) |> validate_required([:name]) |> cast_assoc(:members, with: &Member.changeset/2, sort_param: :members_sort, drop_param: :members_drop ) end The `:sort_param` and `:drop_param` options are what makes dynamic add/remove work without manual index juggling. Ecto reads a `members_sort` array from the params (each element is either a numeric index into the existing rows or `"new"` for a fresh row) and builds the new collection from that. The `members_drop` array lists indices to delete. ### 6.3 Template — parent form with dynamic child rows <.form for={@form} phx-change="validate" phx-submit="save" phx-target={@myself}> <.input field={@form[:name]} label="Team name" />

Members

<.inputs_for :let={mf} field={@form[:members]}>
<.input field={mf[:name]} label="Name" /> <.input field={mf[:email]} label="Email" />
<.button type="submit">Save team The trailing `` ensures the `members_drop` key is always present in params, even if no row is being removed — otherwise `cast_assoc` can't tell the difference between "nothing dropped" and "drop all". ### 6.4 Why put the form in a LiveComponent? Lifting a complex nested form into the parent LiveView is tempting but wrong. See §2. The form owns its own state — changeset, form struct, error messages, the currently-focused row — and the parent doesn't need to re-render every time the user types. Create a `TeamFormComponent`, pass the team in as a prop, and let it own `handle_event("validate")` and `handle_event("save")`. The parent just receives `{:team_saved, team}` via `send(self(), ...)` and navigates. ## 7. Streams and collections `Phoenix.LiveView.stream/3` is the right choice for any collection over ~100 items or any list that grows via realtime updates. Streams live under `@streams.` (not regular assigns) and LiveView manages their DOM identity so individual inserts and deletes don't force a full re-render. ### 7.1 Basic stream def mount(_params, _session, socket) do {:ok, stream(socket, :messages, Chat.list_messages())} end def render(assigns) do ~H"""
<%= msg.body %>
""" end Note the two required bits: 1. The outer container has `id="messages"` and `phx-update="stream"`. 2. Each child element has `id={dom_id}` where `dom_id` is the first element of the `{dom_id, msg}` tuple. Without these, the stream machinery can't track which DOM nodes correspond to which stream items. ### 7.2 Mutating a stream def handle_info({:new_message, msg}, socket) do {:noreply, stream_insert(socket, :messages, msg, at: 0)} end def handle_info({:message_updated, msg}, socket) do {:noreply, stream_insert(socket, :messages, msg)} # same id -> update in place end def handle_info({:message_deleted, msg}, socket) do {:noreply, stream_delete(socket, :messages, msg)} end `stream_insert/3` uses the item's `id` field (or whatever's configured via `:dom_id` in the initial `stream/3` call) to decide whether to insert a new node or replace an existing one. The `at: 0` option prepends the new row. ### 7.3 Reset on filter / sort / search When the entire collection changes (user applied a filter, sorted a column, typed in a search box), you cannot diff — LiveView has no way to know which old rows should disappear and which new rows are updates. Use `reset: true`: def handle_params(params, _uri, socket) do items = Catalog.list_items(params) {:noreply, stream(socket, :items, items, reset: true)} end The `reset: true` option clears the stream's internal state and replaces everything in one pass. Use it whenever the collection identity changes. ### 7.4 Stream vs assign — when to use which - **Use stream** for: collections over ~100 items, realtime feeds, chat messages, notifications, audit logs, large tables, anything that grows. - **Use assign** for: small fixed lists (nav items, tabs, category filters, dropdown options), anything under ~50 items, anything you need to `Enum.count/1` or `Enum.find/2` on. Streams are opaque — you cannot `Enum.count/1` `@streams.messages`. Track the length in a separate assign (`assign(socket, :message_count, n)`) if you need it in the template. ## 8. Mount + lifecycle A LiveView process has two phases on first load: 1. **HTTP mount.** The LiveView runs `mount/3` and `render/1` synchronously over HTTP, sends the rendered HTML back, and terminates the HTTP process. The user sees the page. 2. **WebSocket mount.** The browser opens a WebSocket connection, and the LiveView runs `mount/3` AGAIN as a long-lived process. The socket is now `connected?(socket) == true`. **This means `mount/3` runs twice.** Any database query in mount runs twice on first page load — once over HTTP, once over WebSocket. This is the #1 source of performance bugs in LiveView. ### 8.1 The `connected?(socket)` guard For side effects that should only happen once (PubSub subscriptions, periodic timers, analytics tracking), guard with `connected?(socket)`: def mount(%{"id" => id}, _session, socket) do if connected?(socket) do Phoenix.PubSub.subscribe(MyApp.PubSub, "room:#{id}") :timer.send_interval(5_000, :tick) end {:ok, assign(socket, :room_id, id)} end ### 8.2 `handle_params/3` vs `mount/3` Put data-loading logic in `handle_params/3`, not `mount/3`. `handle_params/3` runs on initial mount AND on every `push_patch`. If the user patches from `/users?sort=name` to `/users?sort=email`, `handle_params/3` fires and can re-query with the new sort. `mount/3` does not fire again. def mount(_params, _session, socket) do {:ok, socket} end def handle_params(params, _uri, socket) do sort = params["sort"] || "name" users = Accounts.list_users(sort: sort) {:noreply, assign(socket, users: users, sort: sort)} end ### 8.3 `assign_async/3` for non-blocking loads `assign_async/3` (LiveView 0.20+) runs a function in a Task, renders a loading state immediately, and resolves the assign when the task finishes. The template branches on three flags: `@users.loading`, `@users.ok?`, `@users.failed`. def mount(_params, _session, socket) do {:ok, socket |> assign_async(:users, fn -> {:ok, %{users: Accounts.list_users()}} end) |> assign_async(:stats, fn -> {:ok, %{stats: Accounts.summary()}} end)} end def render(assigns) do ~H"""
Loading users...
Failed to load users.
<.user_list :if={@users.ok?} users={@users.result} />
Loading stats...
<.stats_panel :if={@stats.ok?} stats={@stats.result} /> """ end Each `assign_async/3` resolves on its own timeline — users load fast, stats load slowly, the user sees each section populate independently. ### 8.4 Gotcha — don't close over the socket Bad: def mount(_, _, socket) do {:ok, assign_async(socket, :user, fn -> id = socket.assigns.current_user.id # closes over entire socket! {:ok, %{user: Accounts.get!(id)}} end)} end The closure captures the entire socket — a huge map. It gets copied into the Task process, wasting memory and risking stale references. Extract the primitives first: def mount(_, _, socket) do user_id = socket.assigns.current_user.id {:ok, assign_async(socket, :user, fn -> {:ok, %{user: Accounts.get!(user_id)}} end)} end ## 9. Event handling Phoenix LiveView has two callback families for events: - **`handle_event/3`** — user-initiated events from the browser (`phx-click`, `phx-change`, `phx-submit`, `phx-keydown`). - **`handle_info/2`** — process-level messages (PubSub broadcasts, `send/2` calls, timer ticks). Both can mutate the socket. Without discipline, state-change logic gets scattered across a dozen clauses that each do a little bit of parsing, a little bit of querying, a little bit of assigning. ### 9.1 The event-to-action funnel Route every state change through a single private function called `handle_action/2`. Each `handle_event/3` clause does nothing but parse the params into a typed struct and delegate. defmodule MyAppWeb.RoomLive do use MyAppWeb, :live_view defmodule Action do defstruct [:type, :payload] end def handle_event("save", %{"message" => %{"body" => body}}, socket) do handle_action(%Action{type: :save, payload: %{body: body}}, socket) end def handle_event("delete", %{"id" => id}, socket) do handle_action(%Action{type: :delete, payload: %{id: String.to_integer(id)}}, socket) end def handle_info({:new_message, msg}, socket) do handle_action(%Action{type: :received, payload: %{message: msg}}, socket) end defp handle_action(%Action{type: :save, payload: %{body: body}}, socket) do case Chat.create_message(%{body: body, room_id: socket.assigns.room_id}) do {:ok, _msg} -> {:noreply, socket} {:error, cs} -> {:noreply, assign(socket, :form, to_form(cs))} end end defp handle_action(%Action{type: :delete, payload: %{id: id}}, socket) do Chat.delete_message(id) {:noreply, socket} end defp handle_action(%Action{type: :received, payload: %{message: msg}}, socket) do {:noreply, stream_insert(socket, :messages, msg, at: 0)} end end ### 9.2 Why this pattern - **Unit testable.** `handle_action/2` takes a socket-like struct and an Action and returns `{:noreply, socket}`. You can test it with plain ExUnit — no `LiveViewTest`, no `conn |> live(...)` boilerplate. - **One place to look.** All state transitions live in `handle_action/2`. Reading a LiveView module becomes: "parse params here, dispatch here, business logic here". - **Consistent between browser and server events.** `handle_event` and `handle_info` both funnel into the same dispatcher. PubSub broadcasts and user clicks get the same treatment. ### 9.3 When to split Keep `handle_action/2` under ~10 clauses per module. Beyond that, split the LiveView — usually by pushing a related cluster of actions into a dedicated LiveComponent. See §2. ## 10. PubSub + realtime Phoenix.PubSub is the message bus for fanning out events across processes. LiveView uses it for realtime updates: user A posts a message, the context broadcasts, every subscribed LiveView for that chat room gets the message in its `handle_info/2`, and each one's template updates via `stream_insert`. ### 10.1 Context-owned broadcast — the single-source rule Every broadcast lives inside the Ecto context next to the write that triggered it. Not inside `handle_event/3`, not in a controller, not in an Oban job. One function, one side effect, one broadcast. defmodule MyApp.Chat do alias MyApp.Chat.Message alias MyApp.Repo def create_message(attrs) do %Message{} |> Message.changeset(attrs) |> Repo.insert() |> broadcast(:message_created) end def update_message(%Message{} = msg, attrs) do msg |> Message.changeset(attrs) |> Repo.update() |> broadcast(:message_updated) end def delete_message(%Message{} = msg) do msg |> Repo.delete() |> broadcast(:message_deleted) end defp broadcast({:ok, %Message{room_id: id} = msg}, event) do Phoenix.PubSub.broadcast(MyApp.PubSub, "room:#{id}", {event, msg}) {:ok, msg} end defp broadcast({:error, _} = err, _event), do: err end The `defp broadcast/2` helper takes an `{:ok, result}` tuple and fans out. The `{:error, _}` clause short-circuits — never broadcast on failure. ### 10.2 Subscribing in LiveView In the parent LiveView's `mount/3`, subscribe only when connected: def mount(%{"id" => id}, _session, socket) do if connected?(socket) do Phoenix.PubSub.subscribe(MyApp.PubSub, "room:#{id}") end {:ok, socket |> assign(:room_id, id) |> stream(:messages, Chat.list_messages(id))} end def handle_info({:message_created, msg}, socket) do {:noreply, stream_insert(socket, :messages, msg, at: 0)} end def handle_info({:message_updated, msg}, socket) do {:noreply, stream_insert(socket, :messages, msg)} end def handle_info({:message_deleted, msg}, socket) do {:noreply, stream_delete(socket, :messages, msg)} end ### 10.3 Topic naming Topics are strings. Keep them consistent across the app: - Per-resource: `"room:123"`, `"post:42"`, `"user:#{user.id}"` - Per-scope: `"rooms"`, `"notifications"` - Per-user-per-resource: `"user:123:rooms"` for private broadcasts Prefer underscored prefixes for domain isolation in larger apps: `"chat:room:123"`. ### 10.4 Routing broadcasts into LiveComponents When a PubSub message arrives at the parent LiveView but the state lives in a child LiveComponent, use `send_update/2` to push it into the child: def handle_info({:member_added, member}, socket) do send_update(MyAppWeb.MemberList, id: "members-#{socket.assigns.team.id}", new_member: member) {:noreply, socket} end ### 10.5 Phoenix.Presence basics `Phoenix.Presence` tracks who's online in a topic. It's built on PubSub and CRDTs, so it handles node joins and partitions gracefully. defmodule MyAppWeb.Presence do use Phoenix.Presence, otp_app: :my_app, pubsub_server: MyApp.PubSub end # In LiveView mount: def mount(%{"id" => id}, %{"user_id" => user_id}, socket) do if connected?(socket) do Phoenix.PubSub.subscribe(MyApp.PubSub, "room:#{id}") {:ok, _} = MyAppWeb.Presence.track(self(), "room:#{id}", user_id, %{}) end {:ok, assign(socket, :present, MyAppWeb.Presence.list("room:#{id}"))} end def handle_info(%{event: "presence_diff"}, socket) do {:noreply, assign(socket, :present, MyAppWeb.Presence.list("room:#{socket.assigns.room_id}"))} end ## 11. Navigation patterns LiveView has three navigation primitives, and choosing the wrong one is the second most common bug (after DB queries in mount). ### 11.1 Decision tree - **Same LiveView module, URL change?** → `push_patch` / `<.link patch={}>`. Fires `handle_params/3` in the current LiveView. No remount. - **Different LiveView module?** → `push_navigate` / `<.link navigate={}>`. Unmounts the current LiveView, mounts the new one fresh. - **Full page reload or external URL?** → `<.link href={}>`. Leaves LiveView entirely. ### 11.2 `<.link>` with both attrs # Same-LiveView sort change — patch <.link patch={~p"/users?sort=name"}>Sort by name # Cross-LiveView drill-down — navigate <.link navigate={~p"/users/#{user}"}>View user # External or reload <.link href="https://docs.example.com">Docs <.link href={~p"/logout"} method="delete">Log out ### 11.3 Imperative navigation in event handlers def handle_event("sort", %{"by" => field}, socket) do {:noreply, push_patch(socket, to: ~p"/users?sort=#{field}")} end def handle_event("view", %{"id" => id}, socket) do {:noreply, push_navigate(socket, to: ~p"/users/#{id}")} end def handle_params(%{"sort" => field}, _uri, socket) do users = Accounts.list_users(sort: field) {:noreply, assign(socket, users: users, sort: field)} end ### 11.4 URL-is-state philosophy The URL is the single source of truth for view state. Filter values, selected tab, current page, sort column — all of them live in the query string, not in assigns. `handle_params/3` derives assigns from the URL. Any user interaction that changes view state calls `push_patch` with the new params, which fires `handle_params/3`, which re-derives assigns. Round trip. Clean. def handle_event("filter", %{"q" => q}, socket) do new_params = socket.assigns.params |> Map.put("q", q) |> Map.put("page", "1") {:noreply, push_patch(socket, to: ~p"/things?#{new_params}")} end ### 11.5 Return-to after login A common pattern: user clicks a link that requires auth, gets redirected to login, logs in, returns to the original page. # Before redirecting to login: socket = put_session(socket, :return_to, current_path(socket)) {:noreply, push_navigate(socket, to: ~p"/login")} # After successful login: return_to = get_session(socket, :return_to) || ~p"/" {:noreply, push_navigate(socket, to: return_to)} ## 12. Auth + authorization LiveView authentication and per-resource authorization both live in `on_mount` hooks, wired via `live_session` in the router. The hook runs before `mount/3`; if auth fails, the hook returns `{:halt, socket |> redirect(...)}` and `mount/3` never runs. ### 12.1 The `on_mount` hook defmodule MyAppWeb.UserAuth do import Phoenix.LiveView import Phoenix.Component def on_mount(:ensure_authenticated, _params, session, socket) do case session do %{"user_id" => user_id} -> user = MyApp.Accounts.get_user!(user_id) {:cont, assign(socket, :current_user, user)} _ -> {:halt, socket |> put_flash(:error, "You must log in to access this page.") |> redirect(to: ~p"/login")} end end def on_mount(:scope_post, %{"id" => id}, _session, socket) do user = socket.assigns.current_user case MyApp.Posts.fetch_for_user(id, user) do {:ok, post} -> {:cont, assign(socket, :post, post)} {:error, :not_found} -> {:halt, socket |> put_flash(:error, "Post not found.") |> redirect(to: ~p"/posts")} {:error, :forbidden} -> {:halt, socket |> put_flash(:error, "You don't have access to that post.") |> redirect(to: ~p"/")} end end end ### 12.2 Router wiring scope "/", MyAppWeb do pipe_through :browser live_session :public, on_mount: [] do live "/", HomeLive end live_session :authenticated, on_mount: [{MyAppWeb.UserAuth, :ensure_authenticated}] do live "/dashboard", DashboardLive end live_session :post_scoped, on_mount: [ {MyAppWeb.UserAuth, :ensure_authenticated}, {MyAppWeb.UserAuth, :scope_post} ] do live "/posts/:id/edit", PostEditLive end end ### 12.3 Context-layer authz decision The context function `Posts.fetch_for_user/2` is where the real authz decision happens. It takes the user AND the resource id, and returns `{:ok, post}` only if the user is authorized to access it. defmodule MyApp.Posts do def fetch_for_user(id, %User{role: :admin}) do case Repo.get(Post, id) do nil -> {:error, :not_found} post -> {:ok, post} end end def fetch_for_user(id, %User{id: user_id}) do case Repo.get_by(Post, id: id, user_id: user_id) do nil -> # Distinguish "not found" from "not yours" carefully: case Repo.get(Post, id) do nil -> {:error, :not_found} _ -> {:error, :forbidden} end post -> {:ok, post} end end end ### 12.4 Defense in depth Even though the on_mount hook authorized access, mutations still take the user as an argument at the context layer: def update_post(%Post{} = post, attrs, %User{} = user) do if can_edit?(post, user) do post |> Post.changeset(attrs) |> Repo.update() else {:error, :forbidden} end end This guards against bugs where a LiveComponent accidentally exposes a mutation path that bypasses the hook (e.g., via a PubSub message handler). ## 13. Testing with LiveViewTest `Phoenix.LiveViewTest` gives you synchronous, in-process testing of LiveViews without booting a browser. ### 13.1 Mounting and asserting import Phoenix.LiveViewTest test "renders the users page", %{conn: conn} do {:ok, view, html} = live(conn, ~p"/users") assert html =~ "Users" assert has_element?(view, "h1", "Users") assert has_element?(view, "a[href='/users/new']", "New user") end `live/2` returns `{:ok, view, html}`. `view` is the LiveViewTest handle, `html` is the initial rendered HTML. Use `html =~ "text"` for string contains, `has_element?/3` for selector-based assertions. ### 13.2 Triggering events test "clicking sort patches the URL", %{conn: conn} do {:ok, view, _html} = live(conn, ~p"/users") view |> element("a", "Sort by name") |> render_click() assert_patched(view, ~p"/users?sort=name") assert has_element?(view, "tr:first-child td", "Alice") end ### 13.3 Form submission test "creating a user", %{conn: conn} do {:ok, view, _html} = live(conn, ~p"/users/new") view |> form("#user-form", user: %{name: "Bob", email: "bob@example.com"}) |> render_submit() assert_redirect(view, ~p"/users") end ### 13.4 Stream assertions test "new message appears in the stream", %{conn: conn} do {:ok, view, _html} = live(conn, ~p"/rooms/123") send(view.pid, {:new_message, %Message{id: 1, body: "hello"}}) assert render(view) =~ "hello" assert has_element?(view, "#messages div[id^='messages-']", "hello") end ## 14. Anti-patterns cheatsheet This is the rapid-reference form of the composite skill's anti-pattern catalog. Every entry has a bad example and a good example. ### 14.1 DB queries in mount/3 # BAD def mount(_, _, socket) do {:ok, assign(socket, :users, Repo.all(User))} end # GOOD def mount(_, _, socket) do {:ok, assign_async(socket, :users, fn -> {:ok, %{users: Accounts.list_users()}} end)} end ### 14.2 PubSub.subscribe without connected? # BAD def mount(_, _, socket) do Phoenix.PubSub.subscribe(MyApp.PubSub, "room") {:ok, socket} end # GOOD def mount(_, _, socket) do if connected?(socket), do: Phoenix.PubSub.subscribe(MyApp.PubSub, "room") {:ok, socket} end ### 14.3 Routes.*_path leftovers # BAD <.link navigate={Routes.user_path(@socket, :show, user)}>View # GOOD <.link navigate={~p"/users/#{user}"}>View ### 14.4 Bare %Changeset{} passed to <.form> # BAD <.form :let={f} for={@changeset} phx-submit="save"> <.input field={f[:name]} /> # GOOD # In mount: assign(socket, :form, to_form(changeset)) <.form for={@form} phx-submit="save"> <.input field={@form[:name]} /> ### 14.5 Enum over assigns for lists >100 items # BAD def mount(_, _, socket), do: {:ok, assign(socket, :messages, Chat.list_messages())} # GOOD def mount(_, _, socket), do: {:ok, stream(socket, :messages, Chat.list_messages())} ### 14.6 assign_new for values needing fresh compute # BAD assign_new(socket, :now, fn -> DateTime.utc_now() end) # stale after reconnect # GOOD assign(socket, :now, DateTime.utc_now()) ### 14.7 Legacy navigation helpers # BAD <%= live_patch "Sort", to: Routes.user_path(@socket, :index, sort: :name) %> # GOOD <.link patch={~p"/users?sort=name"}>Sort ### 14.8 LiveComponent without :id # BAD <.live_component module={FilterPanel} users={@users} /> # GOOD <.live_component module={FilterPanel} id="filter-main" users={@users} /> ### 14.9 Repo calls inside function components # BAD def user_card(assigns) do user = Repo.get(User, assigns.user_id) ~H"
<%= user.name %>
" end # GOOD — parent passes the user in attr :user, User, required: true def user_card(assigns), do: ~H"
<%= @user.name %>
" ### 14.10 PubSub.broadcast called from handle_event # BAD def handle_event("save", params, socket) do {:ok, msg} = Repo.insert(Message.changeset(%Message{}, params)) Phoenix.PubSub.broadcast(MyApp.PubSub, "room:1", {:new, msg}) {:noreply, socket} end # GOOD — context owns the broadcast def handle_event("save", params, socket) do {:ok, _msg} = Chat.create_message(params) # broadcasts internally {:noreply, socket} end ### 14.11 Props assigned outside update/2 # BAD def handle_event("reload", _, socket) do {:noreply, assign(socket, :user, socket.assigns.user)} end # GOOD — props only flow through update/2 def handle_event("reload", _, socket) do send(self(), {:reload_user, socket.assigns.user.id}) {:noreply, socket} end ### 14.12 Raw params map stuffed into %Action{} # BAD def handle_event("save", params, socket) do handle_action(%Action{type: :save, payload: params}, socket) end # GOOD def handle_event("save", %{"message" => %{"body" => body}}, socket) do handle_action(%Action{type: :save, payload: %{body: body}}, socket) end ### 14.13 Re-fetching after on_mount scoped # BAD — on_mount already put the post in assigns def mount(%{"id" => id}, _, socket) do post = Posts.get_post!(id) # wasted query, TOCTOU risk {:ok, assign(socket, :post, post)} end # GOOD def mount(_, _, socket) do {:ok, socket} # socket.assigns.post already set by on_mount end ### 14.14 Template <%= for %> instead of :for= # BAD <%= for user <- @users do %>
  • <%= user.name %>
    • <% end %> # GOOD
  • <%= user.name %>
    • --- End of guide. For the quick-reference rules and detector commands, see the composite `SKILL.md`. For the canonical Phoenix docs, see `https://hexdocs.pm/phoenix_live_view/`.
supporting_files.scripts/main_helper.py:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
phxlv — Phoenix 1.7+ LiveView helper CLI.

A small but real diagnostic and scaffolding tool for Phoenix LiveView codebases.
It implements three subcommands that operationalize the patterns taught in the
elixir-phoenix-liveview composite skill:

    scan <path>        Walk an Elixir tree and flag anti-patterns taken from
                       the LiveView composite's Anti-Patterns Catalog. Output
                       is a gcc-style `file:line:col: pattern — fix`.

    migrate <file>     Rewrite a Phoenix 1.6 LiveView file to 1.7+ idioms.
                       Handles Routes.*_path -> ~p sigils, live_link /
                       live_patch / live_redirect -> <.link navigate|patch>,
                       push_redirect -> push_navigate, ~L -> ~H, and the
                       simple wrapped-tag cases of <%= for %> / <%= if %> ->
                       :for= / :if=.

    new-live <name>    Scaffold a new LiveView module that is a complete,
                       compilable Phoenix 1.7+ example (mount with a stream,
                       handle_params, event funnel, render with ~H, verified
                       routes, :for and :if directives).

Exit codes are meaningful: scan returns 0 when clean, 1 when any hit is found;
migrate returns 0 when at least one rewrite was applied, 1 when the input is
already clean; new-live returns 0 on success.

Usage:
    python main_helper.py scan lib/
    python main_helper.py migrate lib/my_app_web/live/user_live.ex
    python main_helper.py new-live dashboard --dir lib/my_app_web/live/
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import re
import sys
from dataclasses import dataclass
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Callable, Iterable, Iterator, Pattern


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Data types
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class Hit:
    """A single anti-pattern match in a file."""

    file: Path
    line: int
    col: int
    pattern: str
    fix: str

    def format(self) -> str:
        try:
            rel = self.file.relative_to(Path.cwd())
        except ValueError:
            rel = self.file
        return f"{rel}:{self.line}:{self.col}: {self.pattern} — {self.fix}"


@dataclass
class Detector:
    """An anti-pattern detector.

    The callable receives the full source text and yields (line, col) tuples
    for every match. This lets detectors use multi-line regex, heuristics, or
    window scans instead of a single one-shot regex.
    """

    name: str
    fix: str
    extensions: tuple[str, ...]  # e.g. (".ex",) or (".ex", ".heex")
    finder: Callable[[str], Iterable[tuple[int, int]]]


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Detector helpers
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------


def _offsets_to_linecol(source: str, offsets: Iterable[int]) -> Iterator[tuple[int, int]]:
    """Convert absolute character offsets into 1-based (line, col)."""
    targets = sorted(set(offsets))
    if not targets:
        return
    line = 1
    col = 1
    idx = 0
    for target in targets:
        while idx < target and idx < len(source):
            ch = source[idx]
            if ch == "\n":
                line += 1
                col = 1
            else:
                col += 1
            idx += 1
        yield line, col


def _finditer(pattern: Pattern[str], source: str) -> Iterator[tuple[int, int]]:
    """Yield (line, col) for every regex hit in `source`."""
    offsets = [m.start() for m in pattern.finditer(source)]
    yield from _offsets_to_linecol(source, offsets)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 14 anti-pattern detectors (from the composite's catalog)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------


# 1. DB queries inside def mount/3
_MOUNT_DB_RE = re.compile(
    r"def\s+mount\s*\([^)]*\)[^\n]*do\b(?P<body>.*?)\bend\b",
    re.DOTALL,
)
_REPO_CALL_RE = re.compile(r"\bRepo\.(all|one|get|get_by|preload|aggregate)\s*\(")


def _detect_db_in_mount(source: str) -> Iterator[tuple[int, int]]:
    for match in _MOUNT_DB_RE.finditer(source):
        body = match.group("body") or ""
        body_start = match.start("body")
        for repo in _REPO_CALL_RE.finditer(body):
            absolute = body_start + repo.start()
            yield from _offsets_to_linecol(source, [absolute])


# 2. PubSub.subscribe without connected? guard
_PUBSUB_SUB_RE = re.compile(r"Phoenix\.PubSub\.subscribe\s*\(")


def _detect_unguarded_pubsub(source: str) -> Iterator[tuple[int, int]]:
    lines = source.splitlines()
    for lineno, line in enumerate(lines, start=1):
        m = _PUBSUB_SUB_RE.search(line)
        if not m:
            continue
        # Look backwards up to 3 lines for a connected?(socket) guard.
        start = max(0, lineno - 4)
        window = "\n".join(lines[start:lineno])
        if "connected?" in window:
            continue
        yield lineno, m.start() + 1


# 3. Routes.*_path / Routes.*_url leftovers
_ROUTES_HELPER_RE = re.compile(r"\bRoutes\.\w+_(path|url)\s*\(")


def _detect_routes_helper(source: str) -> Iterator[tuple[int, int]]:
    yield from _finditer(_ROUTES_HELPER_RE, source)


# 4. Bare %Changeset{} passed directly into <.form for={...}>
# Matches any <.form ... for={@something}> where `something` does not mention
# to_form — a cheap but practical approximation.
_FORM_RAW_RE = re.compile(
    r"<\.form\b[^>]*\bfor=\{@(?P<ident>[A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*)\}",
)


def _detect_raw_form(source: str) -> Iterator[tuple[int, int]]:
    has_to_form = "to_form(" in source
    for m in _FORM_RAW_RE.finditer(source):
        ident = m.group("ident")
        # If the file already uses to_form/2 and the binding looks like a form
        # (form, f, user_form...) assume it's already wrapped.
        if has_to_form and (ident == "form" or ident.endswith("_form") or ident == "f"):
            continue
        yield from _offsets_to_linecol(source, [m.start()])


# 5. Enum-over-assigns collection that should be stream/3
# Heuristic: a :for iterating `@xs` in a template where the same file also has
# `assign(..., :xs, ...)` and no `stream(..., :xs, ...)`.
_FOR_DIRECTIVE_RE = re.compile(r":for=\{[A-Za-z_]\w*\s*<-\s*@(?P<name>[A-Za-z_]\w*)\}")


def _detect_missing_stream(source: str) -> Iterator[tuple[int, int]]:
    for m in _FOR_DIRECTIVE_RE.finditer(source):
        name = m.group("name")
        assigned = re.search(rf"assign\([^)]*:{name}\b", source)
        streamed = re.search(rf"stream\([^)]*:{name}\b", source)
        if assigned and not streamed:
            # Only flag plural-looking collection names or things explicitly
            # coming from a list_* context function.
            if name.endswith("s") or f"list_{name}" in source:
                yield from _offsets_to_linecol(source, [m.start()])


# 6. assign_new around values that need a fresh compute (Repo / DateTime.utc_now)
_ASSIGN_NEW_RE = re.compile(
    r"assign_new\s*\([^,]+,\s*:\w+,\s*fn\s*->\s*(?P<body>.*?)\bend\b",
    re.DOTALL,
)


def _detect_stale_assign_new(source: str) -> Iterator[tuple[int, int]]:
    for match in _ASSIGN_NEW_RE.finditer(source):
        body = match.group("body") or ""
        if re.search(
            r"\bRepo\.|DateTime\.utc_now|NaiveDateTime\.utc_now|Time\.utc_now",
            body,
        ):
            yield from _offsets_to_linecol(source, [match.start()])


# 7. Legacy nav helpers
_LEGACY_NAV_RE = re.compile(r"\b(live_link|live_patch|live_redirect)\b")


def _detect_legacy_nav(source: str) -> Iterator[tuple[int, int]]:
    yield from _finditer(_LEGACY_NAV_RE, source)


# 8. <.live_component ...> missing an :id assign
_LIVE_COMPONENT_RE = re.compile(r"<\.live_component\b[^>]*/?>", re.DOTALL)


def _detect_live_component_no_id(source: str) -> Iterator[tuple[int, int]]:
    for m in _LIVE_COMPONENT_RE.finditer(source):
        tag = m.group(0)
        if re.search(r"\bid=", tag):
            continue
        yield from _offsets_to_linecol(source, [m.start()])


# 9. Repo calls inside function components (component file has `def x(assigns)`)
_FUNC_COMPONENT_RE = re.compile(
    r"def\s+[A-Za-z_]\w*\s*\(assigns\)\s*do\b(?P<body>.*?)\bend\b",
    re.DOTALL,
)


def _detect_repo_in_component(source: str) -> Iterator[tuple[int, int]]:
    for match in _FUNC_COMPONENT_RE.finditer(source):
        body = match.group("body") or ""
        body_start = match.start("body")
        for repo in _REPO_CALL_RE.finditer(body):
            absolute = body_start + repo.start()
            yield from _offsets_to_linecol(source, [absolute])


# 10. PubSub.broadcast called from inside handle_event/3
_HANDLE_EVENT_RE = re.compile(
    r"def\s+handle_event\s*\([^)]*\)[^\n]*do\b(?P<body>.*?)\bend\b",
    re.DOTALL,
)
_BROADCAST_RE = re.compile(r"Phoenix\.PubSub\.broadcast\s*\(")


def _detect_broadcast_in_handle_event(source: str) -> Iterator[tuple[int, int]]:
    for match in _HANDLE_EVENT_RE.finditer(source):
        body = match.group("body") or ""
        body_start = match.start("body")
        for bc in _BROADCAST_RE.finditer(body):
            absolute = body_start + bc.start()
            yield from _offsets_to_linecol(source, [absolute])


# 11. Props being assigned outside update/2 (in handle_event)
_PROP_ASSIGN_RE = re.compile(
    r"assign\(socket,\s*:[A-Za-z_]\w*,\s*assigns\.[A-Za-z_]\w*\)"
)


def _detect_prop_assign_outside_update(source: str) -> Iterator[tuple[int, int]]:
    for match in _HANDLE_EVENT_RE.finditer(source):
        body = match.group("body") or ""
        body_start = match.start("body")
        for hit in _PROP_ASSIGN_RE.finditer(body):
            absolute = body_start + hit.start()
            yield from _offsets_to_linecol(source, [absolute])


# 12. Raw params stuffed into %Action{payload: params}
_ACTION_RAW_RE = re.compile(r"%Action\{[^}]*payload:\s*params\b[^}]*\}")


def _detect_raw_action(source: str) -> Iterator[tuple[int, int]]:
    yield from _finditer(_ACTION_RAW_RE, source)


# 13. Re-fetching a resource after on_mount scoped it
_ON_MOUNT_SCOPE_RE = re.compile(r"on_mount.*:scope_")
_RESOURCE_FETCH_RE = re.compile(r"\b(Posts|Accounts|Blog|Catalog|Shop)\.get_\w+\(|\bRepo\.get\(")


def _detect_double_fetch(source: str) -> Iterator[tuple[int, int]]:
    if not _ON_MOUNT_SCOPE_RE.search(source):
        return
    for m in _RESOURCE_FETCH_RE.finditer(source):
        yield from _offsets_to_linecol(source, [m.start()])


# 14. <%= for %> / <%= if %> in HEEx templates
_EEX_FOR_RE = re.compile(r"<%=\s*for\s+")
_EEX_IF_RE = re.compile(r"<%=\s*if\s+")


def _detect_eex_for(source: str) -> Iterator[tuple[int, int]]:
    yield from _finditer(_EEX_FOR_RE, source)
    yield from _finditer(_EEX_IF_RE, source)


DETECTORS: tuple[Detector, ...] = (
    Detector(
        name="db-in-mount",
        fix="wrap in assign_async/3 or guard with if connected?(socket)",
        extensions=(".ex",),
        finder=_detect_db_in_mount,
    ),
    Detector(
        name="unguarded-pubsub-subscribe",
        fix="wrap in if connected?(socket) do ... end",
        extensions=(".ex",),
        finder=_detect_unguarded_pubsub,
    ),
    Detector(
        name="legacy-routes-helper",
        fix='rewrite as ~p"/..." verified route',
        extensions=(".ex", ".heex"),
        finder=_detect_routes_helper,
    ),
    Detector(
        name="bare-changeset-form",
        fix="pass changeset through to_form/2 before assigning",
        extensions=(".ex", ".heex"),
        finder=_detect_raw_form,
    ),
    Detector(
        name="missing-stream",
        fix='switch @collection to stream/3 and phx-update="stream"',
        extensions=(".ex", ".heex"),
        finder=_detect_missing_stream,
    ),
    Detector(
        name="stale-assign-new",
        fix="replace assign_new with assign or assign_async for fresh values",
        extensions=(".ex",),
        finder=_detect_stale_assign_new,
    ),
    Detector(
        name="legacy-nav-helper",
        fix='use <.link navigate={~p"/..."}> or <.link patch={~p"/..."}>',
        extensions=(".ex", ".heex"),
        finder=_detect_legacy_nav,
    ),
    Detector(
        name="live-component-missing-id",
        fix="add a stable id= attribute to <.live_component />",
        extensions=(".ex", ".heex"),
        finder=_detect_live_component_no_id,
    ),
    Detector(
        name="repo-in-function-component",
        fix="move Repo calls out of function components; pass data in via attrs",
        extensions=(".ex",),
        finder=_detect_repo_in_component,
    ),
    Detector(
        name="broadcast-in-handle-event",
        fix="move Phoenix.PubSub.broadcast into the context module",
        extensions=(".ex",),
        finder=_detect_broadcast_in_handle_event,
    ),
    Detector(
        name="props-outside-update",
        fix="assign external props only inside update/2",
        extensions=(".ex",),
        finder=_detect_prop_assign_outside_update,
    ),
    Detector(
        name="raw-params-in-action",
        fix="coerce params into typed fields before constructing %Action{}",
        extensions=(".ex",),
        finder=_detect_raw_action,
    ),
    Detector(
        name="double-fetch-after-on-mount",
        fix="trust socket.assigns.resource; drop the re-fetch",
        extensions=(".ex",),
        finder=_detect_double_fetch,
    ),
    Detector(
        name="eex-in-heex",
        fix="replace <%= for/if %> with :for= / :if= directives",
        extensions=(".ex", ".heex"),
        finder=_detect_eex_for,
    ),
)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# scan
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------


def _iter_source_files(root: Path) -> Iterator[Path]:
    for path in root.rglob("*"):
        if not path.is_file():
            continue
        if path.suffix not in {".ex", ".heex"}:
            continue
        parts = set(path.parts)
        if parts & {"deps", "_build", "node_modules", ".git"}:
            continue
        yield path


def _read_text_safely(path: Path) -> str | None:
    try:
        return path.read_text(encoding="utf-8", errors="replace")
    except (OSError, PermissionError) as exc:
        print(f"warning: could not read {path}: {exc}", file=sys.stderr)
        return None


def cmd_scan(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
    root = Path(args.path).resolve()
    if not root.exists():
        print(f"error: path does not exist: {root}", file=sys.stderr)
        return 2

    if root.is_file():
        files: list[Path] = [root]
    else:
        files = list(_iter_source_files(root))

    hits: list[Hit] = []
    for file in files:
        source = _read_text_safely(file)
        if source is None:
            continue
        for detector in DETECTORS:
            if file.suffix not in detector.extensions:
                continue
            for line, col in detector.finder(source):
                hits.append(
                    Hit(
                        file=file,
                        line=line,
                        col=col,
                        pattern=detector.name,
                        fix=detector.fix,
                    )
                )

    hits.sort(key=lambda h: (str(h.file), h.line, h.col, h.pattern))
    for hit in hits:
        print(hit.format())

    if not hits:
        print(f"phxlv: scan clean ({len(files)} files checked)", file=sys.stderr)
        return 0

    distinct_files = len({h.file for h in hits})
    print(
        f"phxlv: {len(hits)} issue(s) across {distinct_files} file(s)",
        file=sys.stderr,
    )
    return 1


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# migrate
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------


@dataclass
class Rewrite:
    name: str
    pattern: Pattern[str]
    replacement: "str | Callable[[re.Match[str]], str]"
    count: int = 0


def _routes_to_sigil(match: re.Match[str]) -> str:
    """Routes.user_path(@socket, :show, id)  ->  ~p"/users/#{id}" (conservative)."""
    prefix = match.group("prefix")
    action = match.group("action")
    arg = (match.group("arg") or "").strip()

    resource = f"{prefix}s"
    if action == "index":
        return f'~p"/{resource}"'
    if action == "new":
        return f'~p"/{resource}/new"'
    if action == "show":
        return f'~p"/{resource}/#{{{arg}}}"' if arg else f'~p"/{resource}"'
    if action == "edit":
        return f'~p"/{resource}/#{{{arg}}}/edit"' if arg else f'~p"/{resource}/edit"'
    if action == "delete":
        return f'~p"/{resource}/#{{{arg}}}"' if arg else f'~p"/{resource}"'
    return f'~p"/{resource}"'


_ROUTES_CALL_RE = re.compile(
    r"Routes\.(?P<prefix>[a-z_][a-z0-9_]*)_path\s*\(\s*@socket\s*,\s*:(?P<action>[a-z_]+)"
    r"\s*(?:,\s*(?P<arg>[^)]+))?\)"
)

_LIVE_LINK_RE = re.compile(
    r'live_link\s+"(?P<text>[^"]+)"\s*,\s*to:\s*(?P<target>[^,)\n]+)'
)
_LIVE_PATCH_RE = re.compile(
    r'live_patch\s+"(?P<text>[^"]+)"\s*,\s*to:\s*(?P<target>[^,)\n]+)'
)
_LIVE_REDIRECT_RE = re.compile(
    r'live_redirect\s+"(?P<text>[^"]+)"\s*,\s*to:\s*(?P<target>[^,)\n]+)'
)
_PUSH_REDIRECT_RE = re.compile(r"push_redirect\(")
_SIGIL_L_RE = re.compile(r'~L"""')

# Simple wrapped-tag <%= for %>...<% end %> rewrite. Only matches a single-line
# wrapper tag followed by `<%= for x <- @xs do %>` block ending with `<% end %>`
# and a matching closing tag. Anything complex is left untouched.
_EEX_FOR_BLOCK_RE = re.compile(
    r"(?P<open><(?P<tag>[a-z][a-z0-9-]*)[^>]*>)\s*"
    r"<%=\s*for\s+(?P<var>\w+)\s*<-\s*(?P<source>@\w+)\s+do\s*%>"
    r"(?P<body>.*?)"
    r"<%\s*end\s*%>\s*"
    r"</(?P=tag)>",
    re.DOTALL,
)

_EEX_IF_BLOCK_RE = re.compile(
    r"(?P<open><(?P<tag>[a-z][a-z0-9-]*)[^>]*>)\s*"
    r"<%=\s*if\s+(?P<cond>[^%]+?)\s+do\s*%>"
    r"(?P<body>.*?)"
    r"<%\s*end\s*%>\s*"
    r"</(?P=tag)>",
    re.DOTALL,
)


def _rewrite_for_block(match: re.Match[str]) -> str:
    open_tag = match.group("open")
    tag = match.group("tag")
    var = match.group("var")
    source = match.group("source")
    body = match.group("body")
    directive = f' :for={{{var} <- {source}}}'
    new_open = open_tag[:-1] + directive + ">"
    return f"{new_open}{body}</{tag}>"


def _rewrite_if_block(match: re.Match[str]) -> str:
    open_tag = match.group("open")
    tag = match.group("tag")
    cond = match.group("cond").strip()
    body = match.group("body")
    directive = f' :if={{{cond}}}'
    new_open = open_tag[:-1] + directive + ">"
    return f"{new_open}{body}</{tag}>"


def _build_rewrites() -> list[Rewrite]:
    return [
        Rewrite("Routes.*_path -> ~p sigil", _ROUTES_CALL_RE, _routes_to_sigil),
        Rewrite(
            "live_link -> <.link navigate=...>",
            _LIVE_LINK_RE,
            lambda m: f'<.link navigate={{{m.group("target").strip()}}}>{m.group("text")}</.link>',
        ),
        Rewrite(
            "live_patch -> <.link patch=...>",
            _LIVE_PATCH_RE,
            lambda m: f'<.link patch={{{m.group("target").strip()}}}>{m.group("text")}</.link>',
        ),
        Rewrite(
            "live_redirect -> <.link navigate=...>",
            _LIVE_REDIRECT_RE,
            lambda m: f'<.link navigate={{{m.group("target").strip()}}}>{m.group("text")}</.link>',
        ),
        Rewrite("push_redirect -> push_navigate", _PUSH_REDIRECT_RE, "push_navigate("),
        Rewrite('~L""" -> ~H"""', _SIGIL_L_RE, '~H"""'),
        Rewrite("<%= for %> -> :for= directive", _EEX_FOR_BLOCK_RE, _rewrite_for_block),
        Rewrite("<%= if %> -> :if= directive", _EEX_IF_BLOCK_RE, _rewrite_if_block),
    ]


def _apply_rewrites(source: str, rewrites: list[Rewrite]) -> tuple[str, list[Rewrite]]:
    out = source
    for rewrite in rewrites:
        def _wrapper(m: re.Match[str], rw: Rewrite = rewrite) -> str:
            rw.count += 1
            replacement = rw.replacement
            if callable(replacement):
                return replacement(m)
            return replacement

        out = rewrite.pattern.sub(_wrapper, out)
    applied = [r for r in rewrites if r.count > 0]
    return out, applied


def cmd_migrate(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
    input_path = Path(args.file).resolve()
    if not input_path.exists() or not input_path.is_file():
        print(f"error: not a file: {input_path}", file=sys.stderr)
        return 2

    source = _read_text_safely(input_path)
    if source is None:
        return 2

    rewrites = _build_rewrites()
    migrated, applied = _apply_rewrites(source, rewrites)

    if not applied:
        print(
            "phxlv: no migration targets found (file is already 1.7+ compatible)",
            file=sys.stderr,
        )
        return 1

    out_path = input_path.with_suffix(input_path.suffix + ".migrated")
    try:
        out_path.write_text(migrated, encoding="utf-8")
    except OSError as exc:
        print(f"error: could not write {out_path}: {exc}", file=sys.stderr)
        return 2

    print(f"phxlv: wrote {out_path}", file=sys.stderr)
    print("phxlv: rewrites applied", file=sys.stderr)
    for rw in applied:
        print(f"  - {rw.name}: {rw.count} match(es)", file=sys.stderr)
    return 0


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# new-live
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------


_LIVE_TEMPLATE = '''\
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Component-forward architecture
#
# The parent LiveView is a thin coordinator. It owns the URL state, session,
# and PubSub subscriptions. Anything interactive should live in a
# Phoenix.LiveComponent under lib/my_app_web/live/components/ so that each
# concern can update independently.
#
# Every handle_event/3 funnels into handle_action/2 so the business logic is
# pattern-matched in one place and unit-testable without LiveViewTest.
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

defmodule MyAppWeb.{camel}Live do
  use MyAppWeb, :live_view

  alias MyAppWeb.CoreComponents

  defmodule Action do
    @moduledoc false
    defstruct [:type, :payload]
  end

  @impl true
  def mount(_params, _session, socket) do
    socket =
      socket
      |> stream(:{snake}s, [])
      |> assign(:page_title, "{title}")
      |> assign(:filter, "")
      |> assign(:sort_by, :inserted_at)

    # TODO: subscribe to domain topics once wired to a context:
    #
    #     if connected?(socket) do
    #       Phoenix.PubSub.subscribe(MyApp.PubSub, "{snake}s")
    #     end

    {{:ok, socket}}
  end

  @impl true
  def handle_params(params, _uri, socket) do
    socket =
      socket
      |> assign(:sort_by, sort_from_params(params))
      |> assign(:filter, Map.get(params, "q", ""))
      |> assign_async(:{snake}s_async, fn ->
        # TODO: replace with your context function, e.g.
        #   {{:ok, %{{{snake}s_async: MyApp.{camel}s.list_{snake}s()}}}}
        {{:ok, %{{{snake}s_async: []}}}}
      end)

    {{:noreply, socket}}
  end

  @impl true
  def handle_event("filter", %{{"q" => q}}, socket) do
    handle_action(
      %Action{{type: :filter, payload: %{{query: q}}}},
      socket
    )
  end

  def handle_event("sort", %{{"by" => field}}, socket) do
    handle_action(
      %Action{{type: :sort, payload: %{{field: String.to_existing_atom(field)}}}},
      socket
    )
  end

  def handle_event("delete", %{{"id" => id}}, socket) do
    handle_action(
      %Action{{type: :delete, payload: %{{id: id}}}},
      socket
    )
  end

  @impl true
  def handle_info({{:{snake}_created, record}}, socket) do
    handle_action(
      %Action{{type: :insert, payload: %{{record: record}}}},
      socket
    )
  end

  # -------------------------------------------------------------------------
  # Pure state transitions — unit-testable without LiveViewTest.
  # -------------------------------------------------------------------------

  defp handle_action(%Action{{type: :filter, payload: %{{query: q}}}}, socket) do
    {{:noreply, push_patch(socket, to: ~p"/{snake}s?q=#{{q}}")}}
  end

  defp handle_action(%Action{{type: :sort, payload: %{{field: field}}}}, socket) do
    {{:noreply, push_patch(socket, to: ~p"/{snake}s?sort=#{{field}}")}}
  end

  defp handle_action(%Action{{type: :insert, payload: %{{record: record}}}}, socket) do
    {{:noreply, stream_insert(socket, :{snake}s, record, at: 0)}}
  end

  defp handle_action(%Action{{type: :delete, payload: %{{id: id}}}}, socket) do
    # TODO: call your context here and broadcast from there, not here.
    {{:noreply, stream_delete(socket, :{snake}s, %{{id: id}})}}
  end

  defp sort_from_params(%{{"sort" => field}}) when field in ~w(name inserted_at updated_at) do
    String.to_existing_atom(field)
  end

  defp sort_from_params(_), do: :inserted_at

  @impl true
  def render(assigns) do
    ~H"""
    <div class="{snake}-live">
      <header class="flex items-center justify-between">
        <h1><%= @page_title %></h1>
        <.link navigate={{~p"/{snake}s/new"}} class="btn btn-primary">
          New {title}
        </.link>
      </header>

      <form phx-change="filter" class="mt-4">
        <input name="q" value={{@filter}} placeholder="Filter..." />
      </form>

      <div :if={{@{snake}s_async.loading}} class="spinner">Loading...</div>
      <div :if={{@{snake}s_async.failed}} class="error">Failed to load {snake}s</div>

      <ul id="{snake}s" phx-update="stream" class="mt-4">
        <li
          :for={{{{dom_id, record}} <- @streams.{snake}s}}
          :if={{record != nil}}
          id={{dom_id}}
          class="row"
        >
          <.link patch={{~p"/{snake}s?sort=name"}}>
            <%= record[:name] || dom_id %>
          </.link>
          <button phx-click="delete" phx-value-id={{record[:id]}} data-confirm="Delete?">
            Delete
          </button>
        </li>
      </ul>
    </div>
    """
  end
end
'''


def _snake_to_camel(name: str) -> str:
    parts = [p for p in name.split("_") if p]
    if not parts:
        raise ValueError("snake_name must contain at least one character")
    return "".join(p[0].upper() + p[1:] for p in parts)


_SNAKE_RE = re.compile(r"^[a-z][a-z0-9]*(?:_[a-z0-9]+)*$")


def cmd_new_live(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
    snake = args.snake_name
    if not _SNAKE_RE.match(snake):
        print(
            f"error: '{snake}' is not a valid snake_case module name",
            file=sys.stderr,
        )
        return 2

    camel = _snake_to_camel(snake)
    title = " ".join(p.capitalize() for p in snake.split("_"))

    target_dir = Path(args.dir).resolve()
    try:
        target_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    except OSError as exc:
        print(f"error: could not create {target_dir}: {exc}", file=sys.stderr)
        return 2

    target_file = target_dir / f"{snake}_live.ex"
    if target_file.exists() and not args.force:
        print(
            f"error: {target_file} already exists (pass --force to overwrite)",
            file=sys.stderr,
        )
        return 2

    body = _LIVE_TEMPLATE.format(snake=snake, camel=camel, title=title)

    try:
        target_file.write_text(body, encoding="utf-8")
    except OSError as exc:
        print(f"error: could not write {target_file}: {exc}", file=sys.stderr)
        return 2

    line_count = body.count("\n") + 1
    print(f"phxlv: created {target_file}", file=sys.stderr)
    print("phxlv: scaffold summary", file=sys.stderr)
    print(f"  + defmodule MyAppWeb.{camel}Live (use :live_view)", file=sys.stderr)
    print(f"  + mount/3 with empty stream(:{snake}s, [])", file=sys.stderr)
    print("  + handle_params/3 with assign_async", file=sys.stderr)
    print("  + handle_event/3 + %Action{} funnel (filter / sort / delete)", file=sys.stderr)
    print("  + handle_info/2 for PubSub messages", file=sys.stderr)
    print("  + private handle_action/2 (pure state transitions)", file=sys.stderr)
    print('  + render/1 using <.link>, :for, :if, phx-update="stream"', file=sys.stderr)
    print(f"  + {line_count} lines total", file=sys.stderr)
    return 0


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# CLI
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------


def _build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        prog="phxlv",
        description="Phoenix 1.7+ LiveView helper — scan / migrate / scaffold.",
    )
    sub = parser.add_subparsers(dest="command", required=True)

    scan = sub.add_parser(
        "scan",
        help="walk a directory and report Phoenix 1.7+ anti-patterns",
        description="Walk a path and report Phoenix 1.7+ LiveView anti-patterns.",
    )
    scan.add_argument("path", help="directory or file to scan")
    scan.set_defaults(func=cmd_scan)

    migrate = sub.add_parser(
        "migrate",
        help="rewrite a Phoenix 1.6 LiveView file into 1.7+ idioms",
        description="Rewrite a Phoenix 1.6 LiveView file into 1.7+ idioms.",
    )
    migrate.add_argument("file", help="the .ex file to migrate")
    migrate.set_defaults(func=cmd_migrate)

    new_live = sub.add_parser(
        "new-live",
        help="scaffold a new Phoenix 1.7+ LiveView module",
        description="Scaffold a new Phoenix 1.7+ LiveView module.",
    )
    new_live.add_argument("snake_name", help="snake_case module name, e.g. dashboard")
    new_live.add_argument(
        "--dir",
        default="lib/my_app_web/live/",
        help="target directory (default: lib/my_app_web/live/)",
    )
    new_live.add_argument(
        "--force",
        action="store_true",
        help="overwrite an existing file at the target path",
    )
    new_live.set_defaults(func=cmd_new_live)

    return parser


def main(argv: list[str] | None = None) -> int:
    parser = _build_parser()
    try:
        args = parser.parse_args(argv)
    except SystemExit as exc:
        return int(exc.code) if exc.code is not None else 2

    try:
        return int(args.func(args) or 0)
    except KeyboardInterrupt:
        print("phxlv: interrupted", file=sys.stderr)
        return 130
    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — top-level guard
        print(f"phxlv: unexpected error: {exc}", file=sys.stderr)
        return 2


if __name__ == "__main__":
    raise SystemExit(main())
supporting_files.scripts/validate.sh:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# validate.sh — Phoenix 1.7+ LiveView anti-pattern scanner
#
# Scans a target directory (typically the root of a Phoenix project) for
# the 14 anti-patterns catalogued in the elixir-phoenix-liveview composite
# skill. Works with ripgrep when available, falls back to grep -rn.
#
# Usage: validate.sh [path]   (default: .)
# Exits 0 on clean / no project, 1 when any anti-pattern is found.
#
set -euo pipefail

TARGET="${1:-.}"
[[ -d "$TARGET" ]] || { echo "validate.sh: '$TARGET' is not a directory" >&2; exit 2; }

# Color support (TTY only)
if [[ -t 1 ]] && command -v tput >/dev/null 2>&1 && [[ $(tput colors 2>/dev/null || echo 0) -ge 8 ]]; then
  RED=$(tput setaf 1); GREEN=$(tput setaf 2); YELLOW=$(tput setaf 3)
  BOLD=$(tput bold); RESET=$(tput sgr0)
else
  RED=""; GREEN=""; YELLOW=""; BOLD=""; RESET=""
fi

# Scanner selection
if command -v rg >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  SCANNER="rg"
else
  SCANNER="grep"
  echo "${YELLOW}note:${RESET} ripgrep not found, falling back to grep -rn" >&2
fi

# Environment check — only scan if this is a Phoenix project with a lib/.
if [[ ! -f "$TARGET/mix.exs" ]]; then
  echo "${GREEN}PASS:${RESET} no Phoenix project detected at '$TARGET' (no mix.exs), skipping env checks"
  exit 0
fi
if [[ ! -d "$TARGET/lib" ]]; then
  echo "${YELLOW}note:${RESET} no lib/ under '$TARGET', nothing to scan"
  echo "${GREEN}PASS:${RESET} nothing to scan"
  exit 0
fi

echo "${BOLD}Phoenix 1.7+ LiveView anti-pattern scan${RESET}"
echo "target:  $TARGET"
echo "scanner: $SCANNER"
echo

declare -A HITS_BY
TOTAL_HITS=0

# --- helpers --------------------------------------------------------------

# scan <pattern> [glob1] [glob2] ... — print file:line matches under lib/
scan() {
  local pattern="$1"; shift
  if [[ "$SCANNER" == "rg" ]]; then
    local args=(-nP "$pattern" "$TARGET/lib")
    for g in "$@"; do args+=(-g "$g"); done
    rg "${args[@]}" 2>/dev/null || true
  else
    local args=(-rEn)
    for g in "$@"; do args+=("--include=$g"); done
    args+=("$pattern" "$TARGET/lib")
    grep "${args[@]}" 2>/dev/null || true
  fi
}

# filter_out <substring> — drop lines containing substring from stdin
filter_out() { grep -v -- "$1" 2>/dev/null || true; }

# files_with <pattern> [glob1] [glob2] ... — list files containing pattern
files_with() {
  local pattern="$1"; shift
  if [[ "$SCANNER" == "rg" ]]; then
    local args=(-l "$pattern" "$TARGET/lib")
    for g in "$@"; do args+=(-g "$g"); done
    rg "${args[@]}" 2>/dev/null || true
  else
    local args=(-rl)
    for g in "$@"; do args+=("--include=$g"); done
    args+=("$pattern" "$TARGET/lib")
    grep "${args[@]}" 2>/dev/null || true
  fi
}

# proximity_scan <anchor-pattern> <target-pattern> <window> <glob> — for each
# anchor line in a file, print target-pattern hits in the next <window> lines.
proximity_scan() {
  local anchor="$1" target="$2" window="$3" glob="$4"
  local files; files=$(files_with "$anchor" "$glob")
  [[ -z "$files" ]] && return 0
  while IFS= read -r f; do
    [[ -z "$f" ]] && continue
    local nums; nums=$(grep -nE "$anchor" "$f" 2>/dev/null | awk -F: '{print $1}')
    [[ -z "$nums" ]] && continue
    while IFS= read -r n; do
      [[ -z "$n" ]] && continue
      local end=$((n + window))
      local chunk; chunk=$(sed -n "${n},${end}p" "$f" 2>/dev/null || true)
      local probe; probe=$(echo "$chunk" | grep -nE "$target" | head -1 || true)
      [[ -z "$probe" ]] && continue
      local offset; offset=$(echo "$probe" | awk -F: '{print $1}')
      local reported=$((n + offset - 1))
      local linetext; linetext=$(sed -n "${reported}p" "$f" 2>/dev/null || true)
      echo "$f:$reported:$linetext"
    done <<< "$nums"
  done <<< "$files"
}

# report <key> <fix> < stdin ... — record hits and print each with fix hint
report() {
  local key="$1" fix="$2"
  local count=0
  while IFS= read -r line; do
    [[ -z "$line" ]] && continue
    echo "  ${RED}${key}${RESET} $line"
    echo "    fix: $fix"
    count=$((count + 1))
  done
  HITS_BY[$key]=$count
  TOTAL_HITS=$((TOTAL_HITS + count))
}

# --- detectors ------------------------------------------------------------

echo "${BOLD}Running 14 detectors...${RESET}"; echo

# 1. DB queries in mount/3 — Repo.* within 20 lines of def mount in *_live.ex
proximity_scan 'def mount' 'Repo\.(all|one|get|preload)' 20 '*_live.ex' \
  | report "D01-mount-repo" "wrap in assign_async/3 or guard with connected?(socket)"

# 2. Phoenix.PubSub.subscribe without connected? guard
scan 'Phoenix\.PubSub\.subscribe' | filter_out 'connected?' \
  | report "D02-unguarded-subscribe" "wrap in: if connected?(socket), do: Phoenix.PubSub.subscribe(...)"

# 3. Routes.*_path / Routes.*_url pre-1.7 helpers
scan 'Routes\.\w+_(path|url)' \
  | report "D03-legacy-routes" 'replace with verified routes: ~p"/users/#{id}"'

# 4. Bare %Changeset{} passed to <.form> (missing to_form)
scan '<\.form [^>]*for=\{@[a-z_]+\}' | filter_out 'to_form' \
  | report "D04-bare-changeset" "wrap the changeset with to_form/2 before passing to <.form>"

# 5. Collections that should be streams (assign(socket, :x, list_*(...)) in LiveViews)
scan 'assign\(socket, :[a-z_]+, [^\)]*list_[a-z_]+' '*_live.ex' \
  | report "D05-list-not-stream" "use stream(socket, :key, list) for collections; don't hold list in regular assigns"

# 6. assign_new for values that need fresh compute (Repo./utc_now near assign_new)
proximity_scan 'assign_new' '(Repo\.|utc_now|DateTime\.now|Date\.utc_today)' 4 '*.ex' \
  | report "D06-stale-assign-new" "use assign/3 or assign_async/3 — assign_new runs only when the key is missing"

# 7. Legacy navigation helpers removed in 1.7
scan '\b(live_link|live_patch|live_redirect)\b' \
  | report "D07-legacy-nav" 'use <.link navigate={~p"..."}> or <.link patch={~p"..."}>'

# 8. <.live_component /> without id= attribute
scan '<\.live_component' | filter_out 'id=' \
  | report "D08-live-component-no-id" "add a stable id= attr; stateful LiveComponents require it"

# 9. Repo.* inside function component modules (components.ex / _components.ex)
{
  scan 'Repo\.' '*components.ex'
  scan 'Repo\.' '*_components.ex'
} | report "D09-repo-in-component" "function components must be pure; move queries to the parent LiveView/LiveComponent"

# 10. PubSub.broadcast inside handle_event bodies (*_live.ex)
proximity_scan 'def handle_event' 'PubSub\.broadcast' 15 '*_live.ex' \
  | report "D10-broadcast-from-event" "move broadcasts into the context module; pipe {:ok, _} writes through a private broadcast/2"

# 11. assign(socket, :prop, assigns.*) inside handle_event — props belong in update/2
proximity_scan 'def handle_event' 'assign\(socket, :[a-z_]+, assigns\.' 15 '*.ex' \
  | report "D11-prop-in-event" "assign props only inside update/2; handle_event mutates component-owned state only"

# 12. <%= for %> inside HEEx / *_live.ex templates
{
  scan '<%=\s*for\s+' '*.html.heex'
  scan '<%=\s*for\s+' '*_live.ex'
} | report "D12-eex-for-in-heex" "replace with HEEx directive: :for={item <- @items}"

# 13. push_redirect/2 (deprecated)
scan 'push_redirect\(' \
  | report "D13-push-redirect" "use push_navigate/2 (push_redirect was deprecated in LiveView 0.18)"

# 14. ~L""" sigil (replaced by ~H in 1.7)
scan '~L"""' \
  | report "D14-tilde-L" 'replace ~L"""..."""" with ~H"""..."""'

# --- summary --------------------------------------------------------------

echo
echo "${BOLD}Summary${RESET}"
echo "-------"
ORDER=(
  "D01-mount-repo" "D02-unguarded-subscribe" "D03-legacy-routes" "D04-bare-changeset"
  "D05-list-not-stream" "D06-stale-assign-new" "D07-legacy-nav" "D08-live-component-no-id"
  "D09-repo-in-component" "D10-broadcast-from-event" "D11-prop-in-event" "D12-eex-for-in-heex"
  "D13-push-redirect" "D14-tilde-L"
)
for key in "${ORDER[@]}"; do
  count="${HITS_BY[$key]:-0}"
  if [[ "$count" -gt 0 ]]; then
    printf "  %s%-28s%s %s%d hit(s)%s\n" "$RED" "$key" "$RESET" "$BOLD" "$count" "$RESET"
  else
    printf "  %s%-28s%s %sclean%s\n" "$GREEN" "$key" "$RESET" "$GREEN" "$RESET"
  fi
done
echo
if [[ "$TOTAL_HITS" -eq 0 ]]; then
  echo "${GREEN}${BOLD}PASS:${RESET} 0 anti-patterns detected across 14 detectors"
  exit 0
else
  echo "${RED}${BOLD}FAIL:${RESET} $TOTAL_HITS anti-pattern hit(s) across 14 detectors"
  exit 1
fi
supporting_files.test_fixtures/db_in_mount_dashboard.ex:
# fixture: LiveView with database queries in mount/3 — the canonical "NO DATABASE QUERIES IN MOUNT" anti-pattern
defmodule MyAppWeb.DashboardLive do
  use MyAppWeb, :live_view

  alias MyApp.Accounts
  alias MyApp.Billing
  alias MyApp.Reports

  # ANTI-PATTERN: every query in here runs twice (once on HTTP, once on WS)
  def mount(_params, _session, socket) do
    current_user = Accounts.get_current_user()
    invoices = Billing.list_recent_invoices(current_user.id)
    metrics = Reports.compute_dashboard_metrics(current_user.id)
    unread_count = Accounts.count_unread_notifications(current_user.id)

    # Another anti-pattern: subscribing without connected?/1 guard
    Phoenix.PubSub.subscribe(MyApp.PubSub, "dashboard:#{current_user.id}")

    socket =
      socket
      |> assign(:current_user, current_user)
      |> assign(:invoices, invoices)
      |> assign(:metrics, metrics)
      |> assign(:unread_count, unread_count)

    {:ok, socket}
  end

  def handle_params(_params, _url, socket) do
    {:noreply, socket}
  end

  def handle_info({:new_notification, _payload}, socket) do
    {:noreply, update(socket, :unread_count, &(&1 + 1))}
  end
end

# fixture: deliberately bad LiveView with 5+ distinct anti-patterns for detection challenges defmodule MyAppWeb.ProjectsLive do use MyAppWeb, :live_view alias MyApp.Projects alias MyApp.Accounts # ANTI-PATTERN 1: DB query in mount/3 # ANTI-PATTERN 2: PubSub subscribe without connected?/1 # ANTI-PATTERN 3: large collection via assign, should be stream # ANTI-PATTERN 4: unscoped PubSub topic def mount(_params, %{"user_id" => user_id}, socket) do current_user = Accounts.get_user!(user_id) projects = Projects.list_all_projects() # 8000+ rows Phoenix.PubSub.subscribe(MyApp.PubSub, "projects") socket = socket |> assign(:current_user, current_user) |> assign(:projects, projects) {:ok, socket} end def render(assigns) do ~L"""

Projects

<%= for p <- @projects do %>
<%= live_link p.name, to: Routes.project_path(@socket, :show, p) %>
<% end %>
""" end # ANTI-PATTERN 5: no authorization check — any user can archive any project def handle_event("archive", %{"id" => id}, socket) do project = Projects.get_project!(id) {:ok, _} = Projects.archive_project(project) Phoenix.PubSub.broadcast(MyApp.PubSub, "projects", {:archived, project}) {:noreply, socket} end # ANTI-PATTERN 6: function head soup def handle_event( "update", %{"project" => %{"id" => id, "name" => name, "owner_id" => owner, "priority" => priority}} = _params, %{assigns: %{current_user: %{id: _user_id}}} = socket ) do Projects.update_project(id, %{name: name, owner_id: owner, priority: priority}) {:noreply, socket} end def handle_info({:archived, _project}, socket) do projects = Projects.list_all_projects() {:noreply, assign(socket, :projects, projects)} end end

# fixture: LiveView mixing push_redirect / live_patch / live_link — all deprecated in 1.7 defmodule MyAppWeb.OrdersLive do use MyAppWeb, :live_view alias MyApp.Orders def mount(_params, _session, socket) do {:ok, assign(socket, :orders, Orders.list_orders())} end def handle_params(%{"filter" => filter}, _url, socket) do orders = Orders.list_orders(filter) {:noreply, assign(socket, :orders, orders)} end def handle_params(_params, _url, socket) do {:noreply, socket} end def render(assigns) do ~L"""

Orders

<%= live_patch "All", to: Routes.order_path(@socket, :index, filter: "all") %> <%= live_patch "Open", to: Routes.order_path(@socket, :index, filter: "open") %> <%= live_redirect "Archived", to: Routes.archived_order_path(@socket, :index) %>
    <%= for order <- @orders do %>
  • <%= live_link order.reference, to: Routes.order_path(@socket, :show, order) %>
    • <% end %>
""" end def handle_event("new", _params, socket) do {:noreply, push_redirect(socket, to: Routes.order_path(socket, :new))} end def handle_event("filter", %{"value" => value}, socket) do {:noreply, push_patch(socket, to: Routes.order_path(socket, :index, filter: value))} end end
