# fixture: LiveView with database queries in mount/3 — the canonical "NO DATABASE QUERIES IN MOUNT" anti-pattern
defmodule MyAppWeb.DashboardLive do
  use MyAppWeb, :live_view

  alias MyApp.Accounts
  alias MyApp.Billing
  alias MyApp.Reports

  # ANTI-PATTERN: every query in here runs twice (once on HTTP, once on WS)
  def mount(_params, _session, socket) do
    current_user = Accounts.get_current_user()
    invoices = Billing.list_recent_invoices(current_user.id)
    metrics = Reports.compute_dashboard_metrics(current_user.id)
    unread_count = Accounts.count_unread_notifications(current_user.id)

    # Another anti-pattern: subscribing without connected?/1 guard
    Phoenix.PubSub.subscribe(MyApp.PubSub, "dashboard:#{current_user.id}")

    socket =
      socket
      |> assign(:current_user, current_user)
      |> assign(:invoices, invoices)
      |> assign(:metrics, metrics)
      |> assign(:unread_count, unread_count)

    {:ok, socket}
  end

  def handle_params(_params, _url, socket) do
    {:noreply, socket}
  end

  def handle_info({:new_notification, _payload}, socket) do
    {:noreply, update(socket, :unread_count, &(&1 + 1))}
  end
end

""" end # ANTI-PATTERN 5: no authorization check — any user can archive any project def handle_event("archive", %{"id" => id}, socket) do project = Projects.get_project!(id) {:ok, _} = Projects.archive_project(project) Phoenix.PubSub.broadcast(MyApp.PubSub, "projects", {:archived, project}) {:noreply, socket} end # ANTI-PATTERN 6: function head soup def handle_event( "update", %{"project" => %{"id" => id, "name" => name, "owner_id" => owner, "priority" => priority}} = _params, %{assigns: %{current_user: %{id: _user_id}}} = socket ) do Projects.update_project(id, %{name: name, owner_id: owner, priority: priority}) {:noreply, socket} end def handle_info({:archived, _project}, socket) do projects = Projects.list_all_projects() {:noreply, assign(socket, :projects, projects)} end end

# fixture: LiveView mixing push_redirect / live_patch / live_link — all deprecated in 1.7 defmodule MyAppWeb.OrdersLive do use MyAppWeb, :live_view alias MyApp.Orders def mount(_params, _session, socket) do {:ok, assign(socket, :orders, Orders.list_orders())} end def handle_params(%{"filter" => filter}, _url, socket) do orders = Orders.list_orders(filter) {:noreply, assign(socket, :orders, orders)} end def handle_params(_params, _url, socket) do {:noreply, socket} end def render(assigns) do ~L"""

Orders

<%= live_patch "All", to: Routes.order_path(@socket, :index, filter: "all") %> <%= live_patch "Open", to: Routes.order_path(@socket, :index, filter: "open") %> <%= live_redirect "Archived", to: Routes.archived_order_path(@socket, :index) %>
    <%= for order <- @orders do %>
  • <%= live_link order.reference, to: Routes.order_path(@socket, :show, order) %>
    • <% end %>
""" end def handle_event("new", _params, socket) do {:noreply, push_redirect(socket, to: Routes.order_path(socket, :new))} end def handle_event("filter", %{"value" => value}, socket) do {:noreply, push_patch(socket, to: Routes.order_path(socket, :index, filter: value))} end end
supporting_files.test_fixtures/nested_invoice_form.ex:
# fixture: attempted nested invoice form with broken inputs_for wiring defmodule MyAppWeb.InvoiceLive.Edit do use MyAppWeb, :live_view alias MyApp.Billing alias MyApp.Billing.Invoice def mount(%{"id" => id}, _session, socket) do invoice = Billing.get_invoice!(id) changeset = Billing.change_invoice(invoice) socket = socket |> assign(:invoice, invoice) |> assign(:changeset, changeset) {:ok, socket} end def render(assigns) do ~H""" <.form :let={f} for={@changeset} phx-change="validate" phx-submit="save"> <%= text_input(f, :customer_name) %>

Line items

<%= for li <- inputs_for(f, :line_items) do %>
<%= text_input(li, :description) %> <%= number_input(li, :amount) %>
<% end %> """ end def handle_event("validate", %{"invoice" => params}, socket) do changeset = socket.assigns.invoice |> Billing.change_invoice(params) {:noreply, assign(socket, :changeset, changeset)} end def handle_event("add_line", _params, socket) do existing = Ecto.Changeset.get_field(socket.assigns.changeset, :line_items) || [] new_line = %MyApp.Billing.LineItem{} changeset = socket.assigns.changeset |> Ecto.Changeset.put_assoc(:line_items, existing ++ [new_line]) {:noreply, assign(socket, :changeset, changeset)} end def handle_event("save", %{"invoice" => params}, socket) do case Billing.update_invoice(socket.assigns.invoice, params) do {:ok, invoice} -> {:noreply, assign(socket, invoice: invoice)} {:error, changeset} -> {:noreply, assign(socket, :changeset, changeset)} end end end

Users

<%= live_patch "New user", to: Routes.user_path(@socket, :new), class: "btn" %>
    <%= for user <- @users do %>
  • <%= live_redirect user.name, to: Routes.user_path(@socket, :show, user) %> <%= if user.admin do %> admin <% end %> <%= live_link "edit", to: Routes.user_path(@socket, :edit, user) %>
    • <% end %>
<%= live_patch "Reload", to: Routes.user_path(@socket, :index) %>
""" end def handle_event("delete", %{"id" => id}, socket) do user = Accounts.get_user!(id) {:ok, _} = Accounts.delete_user(user) {:noreply, push_redirect(socket, to: Routes.user_path(socket, :index))} end end
supporting_files.test_fixtures/pubsub_unguarded_chat.ex:
# fixture: chat LiveView missing connected?/1 guard + unscoped PubSub topic
defmodule MyAppWeb.ChatLive do
  use MyAppWeb, :live_view

  alias MyApp.Chat

  def mount(%{"room_id" => room_id}, _session, socket) do
    # ANTI-PATTERN: subscribing without connected?/1 guard
    # ANTI-PATTERN: topic is "messages" — no scoping by room_id, leaks across rooms
    Phoenix.PubSub.subscribe(MyApp.PubSub, "messages")

    messages = Chat.list_messages(room_id)

    socket =
      socket
      |> assign(:room_id, room_id)
      |> assign(:messages, messages)

    {:ok, socket}
  end

  def handle_info({:new_message, message}, socket) do
    {:noreply, update(socket, :messages, &[message | &1])}
  end

  def handle_event("send", %{"body" => body}, socket) do
    {:ok, message} = Chat.create_message(socket.assigns.room_id, body)
    # ANTI-PATTERN: broadcast on unscoped topic
    Phoenix.PubSub.broadcast(MyApp.PubSub, "messages", {:new_message, message})
    {:noreply, socket}
  end
end

frontmatter.description: Explicit list of Phoenix 1.7+ LiveView anti-patterns to avoid.
frontmatter.name: anti-patterns-catalog--iron-laws

skill_genomes.skill_md_content:
---
name: anti-patterns-catalog--iron-laws
description: Explicit list of Phoenix 1.7+ LiveView anti-patterns to avoid.
---

# Iron-Law Catalog

## Iron laws
1. **NO DATABASE QUERIES IN MOUNT** — mount runs twice
2. **Always connected?(socket) before PubSub.subscribe**
3. **Always ~p, never Routes.*_path**
4. **Always to_form for forms, never bare changesets**
5. **Always stream/3 for lists >100 items**
6. **Never use assign_new for values that need fresh computation on reconnect**
7. **Never use live_link, live_patch, live_redirect — use <.link> and push_navigate/push_patch**

frontmatter.description: Phoenix 1.7+ LiveView anti-patterns with mechanical detectors — grep patterns, AST probes, and Credo checks so reviewers can find violations automatically in any codebase.
frontmatter.name: anti-patterns-catalog--detector-rules

skill_genomes.skill_md_content:
---
name: anti-patterns-catalog--detector-rules
description: Phoenix 1.7+ LiveView anti-patterns with mechanical detectors — grep patterns, AST probes, and Credo checks so reviewers can find violations automatically in any codebase.
---

# Detector-Rule Catalog

## Quick Start
Every anti-pattern below ships with a detector: a grep, AST probe, or Credo check you can run against a codebase. Run the detectors top-to-bottom; each hit is a review finding.

## Anti-patterns and detectors

1. **DB queries in mount/3** — mount runs twice; duplicate queries on every page load.
   - Grep: `rg -n 'def mount' -A 20 lib/**/*_live.ex | rg -B1 'Repo\.(all|one|get|preload)'`
   - Fix: wrap in `if connected?(socket), do: load(), else: assign(socket, :items, [])`.

2. **PubSub.subscribe without connected?** — subscribes twice, leaks processes.
   - Grep: `rg -nP 'Phoenix\.PubSub\.subscribe' lib/ | rg -v 'connected\?'`
   - Credo: custom `Credo.Check.Warning.UnguardedPubSubSubscribe`.

3. **Routes.*_path / Routes.*_url leftovers** — pre-1.7 helpers; verified routes are mandatory.
   - Grep: `rg -n 'Routes\.\w+_(path|url)' lib/ test/`
   - Rewrite: `Routes.user_path(@socket, :show, id)` -> `~p"/users/#{id}"`.

4. **Bare %Changeset{} passed to <.form>** — skips `to_form/2`.
   - Grep: `rg -nP '<\.form [^>]*for=\{@(?!.*to_form)' lib/`

5. **Enum over assigns for lists > 100 items** — should be `stream/3`.
   - Heuristic: context function name starts with `list_` and HEEx uses `:for={item <- @items}`.

6. **assign_new for values needing fresh compute** — stale after reconnect.
   - Grep: `rg -nP 'assign_new.*fn ->' lib/ -A 3 | rg -B1 '(Repo|utc_now)'`

7. **Legacy navigation helpers** — `live_link`, `live_patch`, `live_redirect` removed in 1.7.
   - Grep: `rg -nP '\b(live_link|live_patch|live_redirect)\b' lib/`

frontmatter.description: Enforces a strict LiveView architecture: LiveView modules own state, function components are stateless, context modules handle DB. NO DB queries in mount. Use when writing or refactoring Phoenix 1.7+ LiveView code. NOT for Phoenix 1.6 or older.
frontmatter.name: architectural-stance--strict-liveview

skill_genomes.skill_md_content:
---
name: architectural-stance--strict-liveview
description: Enforces a strict LiveView architecture: LiveView modules own state, function components are stateless, context modules handle DB. NO DB queries in mount. Use when writing or refactoring Phoenix 1.7+ LiveView code. NOT for Phoenix 1.6 or older.
---

# Strict LiveView

## Quick Start
LiveView owns state. Function components render. Context handles DB. No DB in mount.

## When to use
- Writing a new LiveView module
- Refactoring a 1.6 LiveView to 1.7
- Reviewing PRs for Phoenix architecture

## Workflow
1. Use `Phoenix.LiveView` for the top-level module
2. Move all DB queries into a context module, called from `handle_params` or `handle_event`
3. Guard side-effectful work with `if connected?(socket), do: ...`
4. Use `to_form/2` for forms, never raw changesets

## Common mistakes
- `Repo.all` in mount (runs twice: disconnected + connected)
- PubSub.subscribe without `connected?` guard
- Using `assign_new` for things that must be freshly computed

frontmatter.description: Component-forward Phoenix 1.7+ LiveView: push stateful UI into LiveComponents, keep the parent LiveView a thin coordinator that routes messages and owns URL state. Use for new LiveView modules, refactors, or PR review. NOT for Phoenix 1.6.
frontmatter.name: architectural-stance--component-forward

skill_genomes.skill_md_content:
---
name: architectural-stance--component-forward
description: Component-forward Phoenix 1.7+ LiveView: push stateful UI into LiveComponents, keep the parent LiveView a thin coordinator that routes messages and owns URL state. Use for new LiveView modules, refactors, or PR review. NOT for Phoenix 1.6.
---

# Component-Forward LiveView

## Quick Start
Parent LiveView is a thin coordinator. Anything stateful lives in a `Phoenix.LiveComponent`. Parent owns URL + session, children own their own slice of state and talk up via `send/2` or `send_update/2`.

## When to use
- Writing a new Phoenix 1.7+ LiveView with more than one interactive region
- Refactoring a bloated LiveView whose `handle_event` clauses exceed ~10 cases
- Reviewing PRs where a single LiveView mutates many unrelated assigns

## Workflow
1. Start the parent `Phoenix.LiveView` with only URL/session state in `mount/3` and `handle_params/3`. No feature assigns here.
2. For each distinct interactive region (form, list, filter, modal), create a `Phoenix.LiveComponent` module under `lib/my_app_web/live/components/`.
3. Give every LiveComponent a required `:id` assign. Render children via `<.live_component module={...} id="..." ... />` — never inline stateful logic in the parent template.
4. Handle user events inside the LiveComponent's own `handle_event/3`. Mutate only that component's socket.
5. When a child must notify siblings or the parent, use `send(self(), {:child_msg, payload})` and handle it in the parent's `handle_info/2`. For targeted child updates, use `send_update/2` with the target component's `id`.
6. Keep all DB work in context modules. Call contexts from the LiveComponent that owns the data, not from the parent.
7. Use `to_form/2` inside the component that owns the form; never lift changesets to the parent.

## Common mistakes
- Hoisting a child's assigns into the parent "just in case" — defeats isolation and forces parent re-renders on every child change
- Omitting `:id` on a LiveComponent (it silently becomes stateless and loses its own assigns between renders)
- Calling `send_update/2` without specifying the component module, or targeting by index instead of a stable `id`
- Mutating child state from the parent via `assign/3` on the parent socket — parents cannot reach into children; use `send_update/2`
- Putting `Repo` calls directly in `handle_event/3` of either parent or component instead of routing through a context function

frontmatter.description: LiveView authentication via on_mount, authorization via per-handle_event checks.
frontmatter.name: auth-and-authz--on-mount-hook

skill_genomes.skill_md_content:
---
name: auth-and-authz--on-mount-hook
description: LiveView authentication via on_mount, authorization via per-handle_event checks.
---

# on_mount Hook + Per-Event Authz

## Workflow
1. Define `on_mount :current_user` hook
2. Use `live_session :authenticated, on_mount: [{MyAppWeb.UserAuth, :ensure_authenticated}]` in router
3. In handle_event, check ownership: `if socket.assigns.current_user.id == post.user_id, do: ..., else: {:noreply, put_flash(socket, :error, "unauthorized")}`

frontmatter.description: LiveView authentication AND per-resource authorization in a single on_mount hook — halt+redirect before mount finishes, so handle_event bodies never repeat authorization checks.
frontmatter.name: auth-and-authz--resource-scope-mount

skill_genomes.skill_md_content:
---
name: auth-and-authz--resource-scope-mount
description: LiveView authentication AND per-resource authorization in a single on_mount hook — halt+redirect before mount finishes, so handle_event bodies never repeat authorization checks.
---

# Resource-Scoped on_mount Authz

## Quick Start
A single `on_mount` hook authenticates the user AND authorizes access to the specific resource identified by `params["id"]`. If either fails, `{:halt, socket |> put_flash(...) |> redirect(...)}` runs before the LiveView ever mounts. `handle_event` bodies contain zero auth logic.

## Workflow
1. Define a two-purpose hook in `MyAppWeb.UserAuth` that authenticates AND fetches the resource scoped to the user.
2. Context owns the authz decision — `Posts.fetch_for_user/2` returns `{:ok, post}` only when the user owns it (or is admin).
3. Wire the hook per-route in the router via `live_session :post_scoped, on_mount: [{MyAppWeb.UserAuth, :scope_post}]`.
4. `mount/3` trusts `socket.assigns.post` — no re-fetch, no re-check. Pass it down to LiveComponents as a prop.
5. `handle_event/3` clauses operate on `socket.assigns.post` directly. No `if current_user.id == post.user_id` checks.
6. For mutations, the context still takes `user` as a defense-in-depth arg (`Posts.update_post(post, attrs, user)`).

## Common mistakes
- Re-fetching the post in `mount/3` after the hook already assigned it.
- Putting the hook on a `live_session` that mixes scoped and unscoped routes.
- Forgetting to handle the `params["id"]` missing case.
- Returning `{:cont, socket}` on authz failure instead of `{:halt, ...}`.
- Letting LiveComponents re-fetch the resource by id from their own assigns.

frontmatter.description: Separates user events (handle_event) from external messages (handle_info).
frontmatter.name: event-handlers-and-handle-info--clear-separation

skill_genomes.skill_md_content:
---
name: event-handlers-and-handle-info--clear-separation
description: Separates user events (handle_event) from external messages (handle_info).
---

# Clear Separation Of Concerns

## Workflow
1. `handle_event/3` for phx-* bindings (click, submit, change, focus)
2. `handle_info/2` for PubSub broadcasts and GenServer messages
3. Never mix — don't send yourself a phx-event

frontmatter.description: Funnel handle_event clauses into tiny dispatchers that call a private handle_action/2 with a typed intent struct. Makes the event-to-state-change path unit-testable without a LiveView harness.
frontmatter.name: event-handlers-and-handle-info--event-to-action-funnel

skill_genomes.skill_md_content:
---
name: event-handlers-and-handle-info--event-to-action-funnel
description: Funnel handle_event clauses into tiny dispatchers that call a private handle_action/2 with a typed intent struct. Makes the event-to-state-change path unit-testable without a LiveView harness.
---

# Event-to-Action Funnel

## Quick Start
Every `handle_event/3` clause is three lines: parse params into an `%Action{}` struct, call `handle_action(action, socket)`, return the result. All state-change logic lives in `handle_action/2`, a pure function you can test with plain ExUnit.

## Workflow
1. Define an `%Action{}` struct at the top of the module: `defstruct [:type, :payload]`.
2. Write one `handle_event/3` clause per `phx-*` binding. Its only job: coerce params into an `%Action{}`.
3. Call `handle_action(action, socket)` and return its result directly. No business logic in `handle_event`.
4. Implement `handle_action/2` as a private function that pattern-matches on `action.type` and returns `{:noreply, socket}`.
5. `handle_info/2` clauses that mutate state also go through `handle_action/2` — convert the message into an `%Action{}` first.
6. Unit-test `handle_action/2` directly with a built socket; no LiveViewTest needed for the core logic.
7. Keep `handle_action/2` under 10 clauses per component — beyond that, split.

## Common mistakes
- Putting `Repo.insert` or `Ecto.Changeset` logic inside `handle_event`.
- Letting `%Action{}` carry raw `params` maps — coerce to typed fields.
- Skipping the funnel for "simple" events — inconsistency destroys testability.
- Forgetting to route `handle_info` state changes through `handle_action/2`.

frontmatter.description: Canonical Phoenix 1.7+ form cycle: to_form, <.form>, <.input>, phx-change validate.
frontmatter.name: form-handling--to-form-canonical

skill_genomes.skill_md_content:
---
name: form-handling--to-form-canonical
description: Canonical Phoenix 1.7+ form cycle: to_form, <.form>, <.input>, phx-change validate.
---

# to_form Canonical

## Quick Start
`to_form/2` wraps a changeset. `<.form for={@form}>` renders. `phx-change="validate"` re-runs validation.

## Workflow
1. `assign(socket, :form, to_form(changeset))`
2. `<.form for={@form} phx-change="validate" phx-submit="save">`
3. `handle_event("validate", %{"thing" => params}, socket)` re-builds changeset and re-assigns
4. Use `<.input field={@form[:name]} />` for fields

frontmatter.description: Nested Phoenix forms via to_form + cast_assoc and <.inputs_for>. Handles has_many associations with add/remove via :sort_param and :drop_param for dynamic child rows.
frontmatter.name: form-handling--nested-assoc-forms

skill_genomes.skill_md_content:
---
name: form-handling--nested-assoc-forms
description: Nested Phoenix forms via to_form + cast_assoc and <.inputs_for>. Handles has_many associations with add/remove via :sort_param and :drop_param for dynamic child rows.
---

# Nested Association Forms

## Quick Start
`cast_assoc(:children)` in the parent changeset unlocks `<.inputs_for field={@form[:children]}>`. Each child renders its own fields; add/remove rows by toggling `:sort_param` and `:drop_param` on the cast_assoc opts — no manual index juggling.

## Workflow
1. In the schema, declare `has_many :children, Child, on_replace: :delete`.
2. In the changeset, call `cast_assoc(:children, with: &Child.changeset/2, sort_param: :children_sort, drop_param: :children_drop)`.
3. In the LiveComponent that owns the parent form, assign normally: `assign(socket, :form, to_form(Parents.change_parent(parent)))`.
4. Render the parent form and iterate children with `<.inputs_for :let={cf} field={@form[:children]}>`.
5. Add a child with a button posting `parent[children_sort][]=new`.
6. Remove a child with `parent[children_drop][]=<index>` plus a hidden input inside the row.
7. On `handle_event("validate", ...)`, rebuild the changeset with `to_form(changeset, action: :validate)`.
8. On `handle_event("save", ...)`, call `Parents.update_parent/2`; on `{:error, changeset}`, re-assign `to_form(changeset)`.

## Common mistakes
- Forgetting `on_replace: :delete` on the `has_many` — removed rows reappear.
- Using `inputs_for` without a matching hidden `sort_param` input — row order scrambles.
- Rebuilding children manually in `handle_event` instead of letting `cast_assoc` + `:drop_param` do it.
- Putting the nested form in the parent LiveView instead of the owning LiveComponent.
- Calling `to_form/2` on the child changeset directly.

frontmatter.description: Phoenix 1.7+ function components with attr/slot declarations.
frontmatter.name: function-components-and-slots--well-typed

skill_genomes.skill_md_content:
---
name: function-components-and-slots--well-typed
description: Phoenix 1.7+ function components with attr/slot declarations.
---

# Well-Typed Function Components

## Quick Start
`attr :name, :string, required: true` declares component inputs. `slot :inner_block` declares children.

## Workflow
1. Define `attr` for every input
2. Define `slot` for named slots and `inner_block`
3. Render via `<%= render_slot(@inner_block) %>`

## Common mistakes
- Missing attr declarations (no type safety)
- Stateful logic in function components (should be LiveComponent)

frontmatter.description: Pure function components — no assigns, no case, no Ecto. Every attr/slot declared; all logic lives in parent LiveView. Rendering-only layer.
frontmatter.name: function-components-and-slots--pure-stateless-layer

skill_genomes.skill_md_content:
---
name: function-components-and-slots--pure-stateless-layer
description: Pure function components — no assigns, no case, no Ecto. Every attr/slot declared; all logic lives in parent LiveView. Rendering-only layer.
---

# Pure Stateless Rendering Layer

## Quick Start
Function components are rendering primitives, not logic holders. `attr :user, User, required: true` in; HEEx out. If you need `case`, a query, or branching business rules, pull it up into the parent LiveView or a context module and pass the result down as a pre-computed attr.

## Workflow
1. Declare every input with `attr :name, :type, required: true` — no untyped assigns allowed.
2. Declare every child region with `slot :inner_block` or named `slot :actions`.
3. Keep the body to HEEx + `render_slot/1` + `~H` interpolation. No `case`, no business branching.
4. If a decision is needed (e.g. badge color for a status), compute it in the parent LiveView's `assign/3` and pass the resolved value as an attr.
5. Co-locate these components under `MyAppWeb.CoreComponents` or a feature-scoped `*Components` module.
6. Test them with `Phoenix.LiveViewTest.render_component/2` passing literal attrs.

## Common mistakes
- Calling `Repo.get/2` or context functions inside a function component.
- Using `case @status do ... end` for class selection — precompute in the parent.
- Accepting a raw `%Ecto.Changeset{}` and branching on `changeset.valid?`.
- Adding `@socket` or `assign/3` calls — function components have no socket.
- Omitting `attr` declarations "just for this one prop".

frontmatter.description: Phoenix 1.7+ HEEx idioms. Uses ~p sigil, :if, :for, <.link>. NOT for 1.6.
frontmatter.name: heex-and-verified-routes--idiomatic

skill_genomes.skill_md_content:
---
name: heex-and-verified-routes--idiomatic
description: Phoenix 1.7+ HEEx idioms. Uses ~p sigil, :if, :for, <.link>. NOT for 1.6.
---

# Idiomatic HEEx + Verified Routes

## Quick Start
`~p"/users/#{id}"` replaces `Routes.user_path(@socket, :show, id)`. `:for={x <- @xs}` replaces `<%= for x <- @xs do %>`.

## Workflow
1. Use `~p` for every route
2. Use `:if=` and `:for=` directives in HEEx
3. Use `<.link navigate={~p"/users"}>` instead of `live_link`
4. Use `~H"""` sigil for templates

## Common mistakes
- Mixing old helpers (`Routes.*`) with new (`~p`)
- Using `<%= for %>` instead of `:for`

frontmatter.description: Stateful LiveComponent with own lifecycle and event handling.
frontmatter.name: live-components-stateful--isolated

skill_genomes.skill_md_content:
---
name: live-components-stateful--isolated
description: Stateful LiveComponent with own lifecycle and event handling.
---

# Isolated LiveComponent

## Quick Start
`use Phoenix.LiveComponent` for stateful children. They get their own `handle_event` and `update/2`.

## Workflow
1. `use Phoenix.LiveComponent`
2. Implement `update/2` for prop changes
3. Implement `handle_event/3` for component-local events
4. Use `send_update/3` to trigger from parent

frontmatter.description: Phoenix LiveComponent with strict update/2 discipline — the ONLY place props become assigns. Use when building stateful LiveComponents that receive props from a parent LiveView and need clean prop-vs-state separation.
frontmatter.name: live-components-stateful--update-discipline

skill_genomes.skill_md_content:
---
name: live-components-stateful--update-discipline
description: Phoenix LiveComponent with strict update/2 discipline — the ONLY place props become assigns. Use when building stateful LiveComponents that receive props from a parent LiveView and need clean prop-vs-state separation.
---

# Update-Discipline LiveComponent

## Quick Start
In a `Phoenix.LiveComponent`, treat `update/2` as the single choke point where external props (`assigns` passed from the parent) are mirrored into socket assigns. Component-local state is derived inside `update/2`, never assigned elsewhere from parent data.

## Workflow
1. `use Phoenix.LiveComponent` and require `:id` in the parent's `<.live_component />` call.
2. Implement `update(assigns, socket)` as the ONLY function that calls `assign(socket, :prop, assigns.prop)` for parent-supplied values.
3. Inside `update/2`, derive any dependent local state and assign it in the same pass.
4. In `handle_event/3`, only mutate component-owned UI state (`:open?`, `:selected_id`). Never re-read props there.
5. When the parent needs to push a change mid-lifecycle, use `send_update(MyComponent, id: "x", prop: new_value)`.
6. For child-to-parent signaling, `send(self(), {:child_event, @myself, payload})` and the parent routes via `handle_info/2`.

## Common mistakes
- Calling `assign(socket, :prop, assigns.prop)` inside `handle_event/3` — props belong in `update/2` only.
- Forgetting `:id` on the parent's `<.live_component />` — stateful components REQUIRE it.
- Deriving filtered/sorted data in the template instead of `update/2`.
- Using `send_update/3` with partial assigns and expecting old props to persist.
- Mutating parent state by reaching "up" through assigns.

frontmatter.description: Enforces the NO DATABASE QUERIES IN MOUNT iron law.
frontmatter.name: mount-and-lifecycle--no-db-in-mount

skill_genomes.skill_md_content:
---
name: mount-and-lifecycle--no-db-in-mount
description: Enforces the NO DATABASE QUERIES IN MOUNT iron law.
---

# No DB In Mount

## Quick Start
mount/3 runs TWICE (disconnected HTTP + connected WebSocket). Putting a Repo call in mount doubles your DB load.

## Workflow
1. Put DB queries in `handle_params/3` (runs once per URL change)
2. For unconditional loads, guard with `if connected?(socket), do: ...`
3. Use `handle_info(:load_data, socket)` + `send(self(), :load_data)` if needed

## Common mistakes
- `def mount(_params, _session, socket), do: {:ok, assign(socket, :users, Repo.all(User))}`
- Using `assign_new` to "dedupe" instead of using `handle_params`

frontmatter.description: LiveView mount lifecycle that uses assign_async for initial data loads. mount/3 starts async tasks and renders a loading state; handle_params/3 triggers reloads. Use when LiveView needs fast first paint without blocking on DB.
frontmatter.name: mount-and-lifecycle--async-load

skill_genomes.skill_md_content:
---
name: mount-and-lifecycle--async-load
description: LiveView mount lifecycle that uses assign_async for initial data loads. mount/3 starts async tasks and renders a loading state; handle_params/3 triggers reloads. Use when LiveView needs fast first paint without blocking on DB.
---

# Async Load In Mount

## Quick Start
`assign_async/3` (Phoenix 1.7+) kicks off a Task during `mount/3`, renders a loading skeleton immediately, then reconciles when the task finishes. First paint is instant; the DB query happens off the LiveView process.

## Workflow
1. In `mount/3`, return `{:ok, assign_async(socket, :users, fn -> {:ok, %{users: Accounts.list_users()}} end)}`.
2. In the template, branch on `@users.loading`, `@users.ok?`, and `@users.result` — render a skeleton while loading, render data when ok?
3. In `handle_params/3`, re-run `assign_async(socket, :users, fn -> ... end)` when URL params change.
4. Wrap the function body in a try/rescue or return `{:error, reason}` from the async fun so the template can show an error state via `@users.failed`.
5. For multiple independent loads, call `assign_async` once per key.

## Common mistakes
- Synchronously calling `Repo.all/1` directly in mount, outside the async fun.
- Forgetting to handle `@users.loading` in the template.
- Using `assign_async` for cheap in-memory assigns.
- Closing over the whole `socket` inside the async fun.

frontmatter.name: navigation-patterns--push-navigate-and-link

name: navigation-patterns--push-navigate-and-link
description: Phoenix 1.7+ navigation: push_navigate, push_patch, <.link>.

# push_navigate And <.link>

## Workflow
1. Cross-LiveView navigation: `push_navigate(socket, to: ~p"/...")`
2. Same-LiveView URL update: `push_patch(socket, to: ~p"/...")`
3. Template links: `<.link navigate={~p"/users/#{user}"}>...</.link>`

frontmatter.description: PubSub subscribe guarded by connected?(socket).
frontmatter.name: pubsub-and-realtime--connected-guard

skill_genomes.skill_md_content:
---
name: pubsub-and-realtime--connected-guard
description: PubSub subscribe guarded by connected?(socket).
---

# Connected-Guarded Subscribe

## Quick Start
Subscribe during disconnected mount wastes subscriptions. Always guard with `connected?`.

## Workflow
1. `if connected?(socket), do: Phoenix.PubSub.subscribe(MyApp.PubSub, "user:#{id}")`
2. `def handle_info({:new_message, msg}, socket), do: {:noreply, stream_insert(socket, :messages, msg)}`
3. Broadcast via `Phoenix.PubSub.broadcast(MyApp.PubSub, topic, {:new_message, msg})`

frontmatter.description: Elixir Phoenix pubsub/realtime pattern — Ecto context owns all Phoenix.PubSub.broadcast calls; LiveView only subscribes. Writes and broadcasts stay co-located so every mutation path fans out identically.
frontmatter.name: pubsub-and-realtime--context-owns-broadcast

skill_genomes.skill_md_content:
---
name: pubsub-and-realtime--context-owns-broadcast
description: Elixir Phoenix pubsub/realtime pattern — Ecto context owns all Phoenix.PubSub.broadcast calls; LiveView only subscribes. Writes and broadcasts stay co-located so every mutation path fans out identically.
---

# Context-Owned Broadcast

## Quick Start
The Ecto context is the single source of broadcast events. Schema writes and `Phoenix.PubSub.broadcast` live side-by-side in one function so every caller (LiveView, Oban job, controller) fans out identically. LiveView only subscribes and handles.

## Workflow
1. In `MyApp.Chat` context, define a private `broadcast/2` helper that fans out `{:ok, result}` tuples.
2. Pipe every write through it: `create_message/1`, `update_message/2`, `delete_message/1`.
3. In the parent LiveView `mount/3`: `if connected?(socket), do: Phoenix.PubSub.subscribe(MyApp.PubSub, "room:#{room_id}")`.
4. Handle events in the parent: `def handle_info({:message_created, msg}, socket), do: {:noreply, stream_insert(socket, :messages, msg)}`.
5. Route into LiveComponents via `send_update/2` when a child owns that slice.
6. Callers in Oban workers or controllers use the same context function — no duplicated broadcast logic.

## Common mistakes
- Calling `Phoenix.PubSub.broadcast` directly from `handle_event/3`.
- Broadcasting before `Repo.insert` succeeds — always pipe the `{:ok, _}` tuple through the helper.
- Making `broadcast/2` public — keep it `defp`.
- Forgetting the `{:error, _}` clause on the helper.
